title.zh: 卷二十三 竇融列傳
title.raw: 卷二十三 竇融列傳 Volume 23: Biography of Dou Rong

sentences.zh: 竇融字周公，扶風平陵人也。

translations.literal: Dou Rong, courtesy name Gong of Zhou, was a man of Pingling in Fufeng.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong, whose courtesy name was Gong of Zhou, came from Pingling in Fufeng commandery.

sentences.zh: 七世祖廣國，孝文皇后之弟，封章武侯。

translations.literal: His seventh-generation ancestor was Guangguo, younger brother of Empress Xiaowen, enfeoffed as marquis of Zhangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Seven generations back, Guangguo, brother of Empress Xiaowen of the Han, had been enfeoffed as Marquis of Zhangwu.

sentences.zh: 融高祖父，宣帝時以吏二千石自常山徙焉。

translations.literal: Rong's great-great-grandfather, in Emperor Xuan's time, as an official of two thousand piculs, moved there from Changshan.
translations.idiomatic: His great-great-grandfather had held a salary rank of two thousand piculs under Emperor Xuan and relocated from Changshan to that region.

sentences.zh: 融早孤。

translations.literal: Rong was orphaned early.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong lost his father while still young.

sentences.zh: 王莽居攝中，為強弩將軍司馬，東擊翟義，還攻槐里，以軍功封建武男。

translations.literal: In Wang Mang's regency, he was marshal to the General of Strong Crossbows, struck east against Zhai Yi, returned and attacked Huaili, and by military merit was enfeoffed as baron of Jianwu.
translations.idiomatic: During Wang Mang's regency he served as marshal to the General of Strong Crossbows, campaigned east against Zhai Yi, then turned back to take Huaili, earning the noble title Baron of Jianwu.

sentences.zh: 女弟為大司空王邑小妻。

translations.literal: His younger sister was a concubine of Grand Minister of Works Wang Yi.
translations.idiomatic: His younger sister had become a concubine of Wang Yi, the Grand Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 家長安中，出入貴戚，連結閭里豪傑，以任俠為名；

translations.literal: The family dwelt in Chang'an; he moved among the powerful kin, linked with village stalwarts, and made a name as a knight-errant;
translations.idiomatic: With their household in Chang'an, he moved in elite circles, cultivated local strongmen, and was known for chivalrous daring;

sentences.zh: 然事母兄，養弱弟，內修行義。

translations.literal: yet he served mother and elder brothers, raised a weak younger brother, and inwardly cultivated conduct and duty.
translations.idiomatic: yet he honored his mother and elder brothers, supported a frail younger brother, and strove in private to live by moral principle.

sentences.zh: 王莽末，青、徐賊起，太師王匡請融為助軍，與共東征。

translations.literal: At the end of Wang Mang, bandits rose in Qing and Xu; Grand Tutor Wang Kuang requested Rong as auxiliary army and with him campaigned east.
translations.idiomatic: Near the fall of Wang Mang, unrest broke out in Qing and Xu provinces; Grand Tutor Wang Kuang secured Dou Rong as auxiliary commander for the eastern expedition.

sentences.zh: 及漢兵起，融復從王邑敗於昆陽下，歸長安。

translations.literal: When Han forces rose, Rong again followed Wang Yi in defeat below Kunyang and returned to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: When Han armies rose in rebellion, Dou Rong followed Wang Yi to defeat at Kunyang and withdrew to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 漢兵長驅入關，王邑薦融，拜為波水將軍，賜黃金千斤，引兵至新豐。

translations.literal: Han troops drove straight into the passes; Wang Yi recommended Rong; he was appointed General Who Crosses the Waters, given a thousand jin of gold, and led troops to Xinfeng.
translations.idiomatic: As Han forces swept into the passes, Wang Yi recommended him; he received appointment as General Who Crosses the Waters, a gift of a thousand jin of gold, and marched to Xinfeng.

sentences.zh: 莽敗，融以軍降更始大司馬趙萌，萌以為校尉，甚重之，薦融為巨鹿太守。

translations.literal: When Mang fell, Rong with his army surrendered to Gengshi's Grand Marshal Zhao Meng; Meng made him a colonel, valued him greatly, and recommended Rong as governor of Julu.
translations.idiomatic: After Wang Mang's collapse Dou Rong surrendered his command to Zhao Meng, Gengshi's Grand Marshal, who made him a colonel, thought highly of him, and proposed him for governor of Julu.

sentences.zh: 融見更始新立，東方尚擾，不欲出關，而高祖父嘗為張掖太守，從祖父為護羌校尉，從弟亦為武威太守，累世在河西，知其土俗，獨謂兄弟曰：『天下安危未可知，河西殷富，帶河為固，張掖屬國精兵萬騎，一旦緩急，杜絕河津，足以自守，此遺種處也。

translations.literal: Rong saw Gengshi newly established and the east still disturbed and did not wish to leave the passes; but his great-great-grandfather had been governor of Zhangye, his father's cousin once removed Colonel Who Protects the Qiang, and a cousin was also governor of Wuwei—generations in Hexi; he knew the land and customs. Alone he said to his brothers: 'The empire's safety or peril cannot be known; Hexi is rich, girded by the Yellow River and secure; Zhangye dependent state has ten thousand picked horsemen; in any crisis, block the river fords and it is enough to defend oneself—this is a place to preserve one's line.'
translations.idiomatic: Seeing Gengshi newly enthroned while the east remained chaotic, Dou Rong was reluctant to march east of the passes. His family had long served west of the river: a great-great-grandfather had governed Zhangye, a cousin's grandfather had been Colonel Who Protects the Qiang, and another cousin governed Wuwei. Generations in Hexi had taught him the terrain and temper of the people. He told his brothers privately, 'We cannot tell whether the realm will stand or fall. Hexi is wealthy, the Yellow River its shield; Zhangye's dependent state fields ten thousand veteran horsemen. In a crunch we could seal the fords and hold our own. Here a lineage can survive.'

sentences.zh: 』兄弟皆然之。

translations.literal: ' The brothers all agreed.
translations.idiomatic: His brothers agreed.

sentences.zh: 融於是日往守萌，辭讓巨鹿，圖出河西。

translations.literal: Rong thereupon daily went to attend on Meng, declined Julu, and schemed to get out to Hexi.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong began calling daily on Zhao Meng, declining the Julu post and angling for an appointment west of the river.

sentences.zh: 萌為言更始，乃得為張掖屬國都尉。

translations.literal: Meng spoke for him to Gengshi, and he became commandant of Zhangye dependent state.
translations.idiomatic: Meng spoke to the Gengshi court on his behalf, and he received appointment as commandant of Zhangye dependent state.

sentences.zh: 融大喜，即將家屬而西。

translations.literal: Rong was greatly pleased and immediately led his household west.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong was elated and at once moved his family westward.

sentences.zh: 既到，撫結雄傑，懷輯羌虜，甚得其歡心，河西翕然歸之。

translations.literal: When arrived, he comforted and tied the bold, cherished and gathered Qiang and foreigners, greatly won their hearts, and Hexi uniformly submitted to him.
translations.idiomatic: Once there he won over local leaders, conciliated Qiang and frontier peoples, and earned their sincere loyalty until the western river lands rallied to him as one.

sentences.zh: 是時，酒泉太守梁統、金城太守厙鈞、張掖都尉史苞、酒泉都尉竺曾、敦煌都尉辛肜，并州郡英俊，融皆與為厚善。

translations.literal: At this time Liang Tong governor of Jiuquan, She Jun governor of Jincheng, Shi Bao commandant of Zhangye, Zhu Zeng commandant of Jiuquan, Xin Ran commandant of Dunhuang—all outstanding of province and commandery—Rong was with all thickly good.
translations.idiomatic: The governors Liang Tong of Jiuquan and She Jun of Jincheng, along with commandants Shi Bao of Zhangye, Zhu Zeng of Jiuquan, and Xin Ran of Dunhuang, were the leading men of the region, and Dou Rong cultivated close ties with each.

sentences.zh: 及更始敗，融與梁統等計議曰：『今天下擾亂，未知所歸。

translations.literal: When Gengshi fell, Rong with Liang Tong and others planned and said: 'Now the realm is in turmoil; it is not known to whom to turn.'
translations.idiomatic: After Gengshi's regime collapsed, Dou Rong and Liang Tong took counsel. 'The empire is in chaos,' they said, 'and no rightful center is clear.'

sentences.zh: 河西鬥絕在羌湖中，不同心戮力則不能自守；

translations.literal: Hexi is cut off like a dou in the midst of Qiang and lakes; if not of one heart and combined strength, cannot self-defend;
translations.idiomatic: Hexi stands isolated among Qiang country and desert; unless we stand together we cannot hold it;

sentences.zh: 權鈞力齊，復無以相率。

translations.literal: weights equal, forces level, again nothing whereby mutually to lead.
translations.idiomatic: and if we are equal in power, no one can command the rest.'

sentences.zh: 當推一人為大將軍，共全五郡，觀時變動。

translations.literal: We ought to push one man as grand general, jointly preserve the five commanderies, and observe the times' changes.'
translations.idiomatic: We should elect one man as grand general, secure all five commanderies together, and watch how the situation shifts.'

sentences.zh: 』議既定，而各謙讓，咸以融世任河西為吏，人所敬向，乃推融行河西五郡大將軍事。

translations.literal: ' When the plan was settled, each modestly declined; all because Rong's family for generations had held office in Hexi and people revered him, then pushed Rong to act as grand general affairs of Hexi's five commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: The plan was set, yet everyone demurred. Because the Dou family had served generation after generation west of the river and the people looked up to Dou Rong, they pressed him to take provisional command as grand general over the five Hexi commanderies.

sentences.zh: 是時，武威太守馬期、張掖太守任仲並孤立無黨，乃共移書告示之，二人即解印綬去。

translations.literal: At this time Wuwei governor Ma Qi and Zhangye governor Ren Zhong both stood isolated without faction; they jointly sent documents informing them, and the two men immediately unsealed seals and cords and left.
translations.idiomatic: The governors Ma Qi of Wuwei and Ren Zhong of Zhangye lacked allies. The coalition jointly notified them in writing, and both men resigned their seals at once and departed.

sentences.zh: 於是以梁統為武威太守，史苞為張掖太守，竺曾為酒泉太守，辛肜為敦煌太守，厙鈞為金城太守。

translations.literal: Thereupon they made Liang Tong Wuwei governor, Shi Bao Zhangye governor, Zhu Zeng Jiuquan governor, Xin Ran Dunhuang governor, She Jun Jincheng governor.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Tong became governor of Wuwei, Shi Bao of Zhangye, Zhu Zeng of Jiuquan, Xin Ran of Dunhuang, and She Jun of Jincheng.

sentences.zh: 融居屬國，領都尉職如故，置從事監察五郡。

translations.literal: Rong dwelt in the dependent state, held commandant duties as before, and set up attendants to supervise the five commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong remained based in the dependent state, kept his former commandant's authority, and appointed staff officers to oversee all five commanderies.

sentences.zh: 河西民俗質樸，而融等政亦寬和，上下相親，晏然富殖。

translations.literal: Hexi folkways were simple and plain, and Rong and others' government also lenient and harmonious; superiors and inferiors were intimate; peacefully they grew rich.
translations.idiomatic: The people west of the river were plain-spoken, and Dou Rong's administration was mild; officials and commoners trusted one another, and prosperity returned in quiet comfort.

sentences.zh: 修兵馬，習戰射，明烽燧之警，羌胡犯塞，融輒自將與諸郡相救，皆如符要，每輒破之。

translations.literal: They trained troops and horses, practiced combat archery, clarified beacon warnings; when Qiang and Hu violated the frontier, Rong always personally led with the commanderies rescuing one another, all as tally agreement, each time crushing them.
translations.idiomatic: They drilled cavalry, honed archery, and kept the beacon lines sharp. Whenever Qiang or Hu raided the frontier, Dou Rong led out in person while the commanderies coordinated relief; their timing matched every plan, and they broke each incursion.

sentences.zh: 其後匈奴懲乂，稀復侵寇，而保塞羌胡皆震服親附，安定、北地、上郡流人避凶饑者，歸之不絕。

translations.literal: Afterwards the Xiongnu were chastened and rarely again invaded; Qiang and Hu who guarded the frontier all trembled submitted and drew close; refugees of Anding, Beidi, and Shang commanderies fleeing disaster famine came to them without cease.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiongnu, duly punished, seldom raided again. Frontier Qiang and Hu submitted and rallied to him. Refugees from Anding, Beidi, and Shang, fleeing war and famine, streamed west without end.

sentences.zh: 融等遙聞光武即位，而心欲東向，以河西隔遠，未能自通。

translations.literal: Rong and others from afar heard Guangwu ascended the throne, but their hearts wished to face east; because Hexi was separated far, could not self-connect.
translations.idiomatic: They learned from afar that Guangwu had taken the throne and longed to declare for the east, yet the distance of Hexi left them no direct channel to the new court.

sentences.zh: 時，隗囂先稱建武年號，融等從受正朔，囂皆假其將軍印綬。

translations.literal: At the time, Wei Xiao first proclaimed the Jianwu era title; Rong and others followed receiving standard calendar; Xiao all lent them general seals and cords.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao was first to use Emperor Guangwu's reign title, and Dou Rong's party accepted his calendar while Wei Xiao issued them general's commissions in name.

sentences.zh: 囂外順人望，內懷異心，使辯士張玄遊說河西曰：『更始事業已成，尋復亡滅，此一姓不再興之效。

translations.literal: Xiao outwardly complied with men's hopes, inwardly harbored different heart, sent persuader Zhang Xuan to lobby Hexi saying: 'Gengshi's enterprise already completed, soon again perished destroyed—this is the proof that one surname does not rise twice.'
translations.idiomatic: Outwardly Wei Xiao courted popularity while plotting his own course. He sent the rhetorician Zhang Xuan west with an argument: 'Gengshi seemed to have won everything, then swiftly vanished—a lesson that no single house rises twice.'

sentences.zh: 今即有所主，便相系屬，一旦拘制，自令失柄，後有危殆，雖悔無及。

translations.literal: Now if there is one you attach to, then mutually tie belong; once restrained controlled, self-order lose handle; afterwards there is peril danger, though regret not reach.
translations.idiomatic: Bind yourself to whatever champion you please and you become his hostage; the moment he clamps down you lose all leverage. Wait until danger closes in and remorse will come too late.'

sentences.zh: 今豪傑競逐，雌雄未決，當各據其土宇，與隴、蜀合從，高可為六國，下不失尉佗。

translations.literal: Now heroes compete chase; male female not decided; ought each occupy their territory, with Long and Shu unite vertical, high can be six states, low not lose Wei Tuo.
translations.idiomatic: Chieftains still vie for mastery; the outcome is open. Hold your own ground, ally Longxi with Shu in the old vertical alliance: at best you replay the Warring States, at worst you end no worse than Zhao Tuo of the south.'

sentences.zh: 』融等於是召豪傑及諸太守計議，其中智者皆曰：『漢承堯運，歷數延長。

translations.literal: ' Rong and others thereupon summoned stalwarts and the governors to plan; among them the wise all said: 'Han inherits Yao's fortune; the succession cycle extends long.'
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong assembled local leaders and the commandery governors. Those with sense replied, 'The Han received Heaven's mandate from the line of Yao; its allotted span is long.'

sentences.zh: 今皇帝姓號見於圖書，自前世博物道術之士谷子雲、夏賀良等，建明漢有再受命之符，言之久矣，故劉子駿改易名字，冀應其占。

translations.literal: Now the emperor's surname and title appear in charts and books; from former generations broad-learned way technique gentlemen Gu Ziyun, Xia Heliang and others, established clarified Han has again receive mandate's sign—spoken long already; therefore Liu Zijun changed names, hoping correspond to its omen.
translations.idiomatic: Prophetic texts already name the present emperor. Scholars such as Gu Ziyun and Xia Heliang long ago argued that Han would receive a second mandate; hence Liu Xin restyled himself hoping to fulfill the omen.'

sentences.zh: 及莽末，道士西門君惠言劉秀當為天子，遂謀立子駿。

translations.literal: Reaching Mang's end, Daoist Ximen Junhui said Liu Xiu ought to be Son of Heaven; then plotted to establish Zijun.
translations.idiomatic: Near Wang Mang's fall the Daoist Ximen Junhui declared that Liu Xiu was destined for the throne; plotters tried to place Liu Xin on the throne instead.

sentences.zh: 事覺被殺，出謂百姓觀者曰：「劉秀真汝主也。

translations.literal: The affair was discovered and they were killed; going out he said to the common people watching: 'Liu Xiu truly is your lord.'
translations.idiomatic: The plot failed and the conspirators were executed; facing the crowd one of them cried out, 'Liu Xiu is truly your rightful ruler.'

sentences.zh: 」皆近事暴著，智者所共見也。

translations.literal: ' All are recent matters violent manifest—what wise ones together see.
translations.idiomatic: These are fresh omens plain for anyone with eyes to see.'

sentences.zh: 除言天命，且以人事論之：今稱帝者數人，而洛陽土地最廣，甲兵最強，號令最明。

translations.literal: Besides speaking Heaven's mandate, and by human affairs discussing it: now those proclaiming emperor are several persons, but Luoyang's territory is broadest, armor weapons strongest, orders clearest.
translations.idiomatic: Leave aside Heaven's verdict and weigh mere facts: several men claim the throne, yet Luoyang commands the widest territory, the strongest armies, and the clearest chain of command.'

sentences.zh: 觀符命而察人事，它姓殆未能當也。

translations.literal: Observe talisman-mandate and inspect human affairs—other surnames perhaps cannot match.'
translations.idiomatic: Read the portents and weigh human strength: no other house can match Liu.'

sentences.zh: 』諸郡太守各有賓客，或同或異。

translations.literal: ' The commandery governors each had retainers; some same some different.
translations.idiomatic: The governors' advisers split both ways.'

sentences.zh: 融小心精詳，遂決策東向。

translations.literal: Rong was careful and refined detailed; thereupon decided policy eastward.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong weighed everything with care and resolved to cast his lot with Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 五年夏，遣長史劉鈞奉書獻馬。

translations.literal: Fifth year summer, sent chief clerk Liu Jun to present letter and offer horses.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the fifth Jianwu year he sent his chief clerk Liu Jun east with a memorial and horses as tribute.

sentences.zh: 先是，帝聞河西完富，地接隴、蜀，常欲招之以逼囂、述，亦發使遺融書，遇鈞於道，即與俱還。

translations.literal: Before this, the emperor heard Hexi intact rich, land connecting Long and Shu, constantly wished to summon them to press Xiao and Shu; also sent envoy leaving Rong letter; met Jun on the road, immediately with together returned.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor had already heard that Hexi remained wealthy and bordered Long and Shu; he meant to win Dou Rong over to squeeze Wei Xiao and Gongsun Shu. An envoy carrying an edict for Dou Rong met Liu Jun on the road and returned with him to court.

sentences.zh: 帝見鈞歡甚，禮饗畢，乃遣令還，賜融璽書曰：『制詔行河西五郡大將軍事、屬國都尉：勞鎮守邊五郡，兵馬精強，倉庫有蓄，民庶殷富，外則折挫羌胡，內則百姓蒙福。

translations.literal: The emperor seeing Jun rejoiced greatly; ritual feast finished, then sent order return, bestowed on Rong imperial letter saying: 'Imperial edict to him who conducts Hexi five commanderies grand general affairs, dependent state commandant: toil guarding frontier five commanderies, troops horses refined strong, granaries treasuries have stores, people masses abundant rich; outward then break Qiang Hu, inward then common people receive blessing.'
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu welcomed Liu Jun warmly, feasted him, and sent him home bearing an imperial rescript: 'We address the officer acting as grand general over the five Hexi commanderies and commandant of the dependent state: you labor to hold the frontier commanderies; your forces are strong, your granaries stocked, your people prosperous; abroad you humble Qiang and Hu, at home your folk thrive.'

sentences.zh: 威德流聞，虛心相望，道路隔塞，邑邑何已！

translations.literal: Might virtue flow heard; empty heart mutually gaze; roads blocked—how end the melancholy!
translations.idiomatic: Your renown reaches us, and we long to meet in open heart, yet the roads are blocked—how we ache with restless grief!'

sentences.zh: 長史所奉書獻馬悉至，深知厚意。

translations.literal: Chief clerk's presented letter offered horses all arrived; deeply know thick intent.
translations.idiomatic: Your memorial and the horses your chief clerk presented have arrived; we deeply appreciate your loyalty.'

sentences.zh: 今益州有公孫子陽、天水有隗將軍，方蜀、漢相攻，權在將軍，舉足左右，便有輕重。

translations.literal: Now Yi province has Gongsun Ziyang, Tianshui has General Wei; just Shu and Han mutually attack; power lies in general; lifting foot left right, then there is light heavy.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Shu holds Yizhou and Wei Xiao holds Tianshui while Shu and Han clash. The balance rests with you: whichever foot you step tips the scales.'

sentences.zh: 以此言之，欲相厚豈有量哉！

translations.literal: By this speaking it, wishing mutually thick how have measure!
translations.idiomatic: If that does not show how much we esteem you, nothing will!'

sentences.zh: 諸事具長史所見，將軍所知。

translations.literal: All matters are detailed in what chief clerk saw, what general knows.
translations.idiomatic: Your chief clerk has seen our full intentions; you understand the rest yourself.'

sentences.zh: 王者叠興，千載一會。

translations.literal: Kings overlap rise; a thousand years one meeting.
translations.idiomatic: True kings arise in succession; an alignment like ours comes once in an age.'

sentences.zh: 欲遂立桓、文，輔微國，當勉卒功業；

translations.literal: If wish then establish Huan Wen, assist tiny state, ought strive finish merit enterprise;
translations.idiomatic: If you mean to play Duke Huan or Duke Wen, sheltering a weak state, then finish the task with all your strength;

sentences.zh: 欲三分鼎足，連衡合從，亦宜以時定。

translations.literal: if wish three divide tripod foot, connect horizontal unite vertical, also ought by time settle.
translations.idiomatic: if you mean to divide the realm three ways and play alliance politics, decide soon.'

sentences.zh: 天下未並，吾與爾絕域，非相吞之國。

translations.literal: The realm not yet united, I with you separated regions—not mutually swallowing states.
translations.idiomatic: The empire is not yet one, yet we stand far apart, not as predators toward each other.'

sentences.zh: 今之議者，必有任囂效尉佗制七郡之計。

translations.literal: Now's debaters, necessarily have Ren Xiao imitate Wei Tuo control seven commanderies' plan.
translations.idiomatic: Someone has surely urged you to copy Ren Xiao and Zhao Tuo seizing seven commanderies.'

sentences.zh: 王者有分土，無分民，自適己事而已。

translations.literal: A king has divided soil, not divided people—self suit own affairs only.
translations.idiomatic: A true king may parcel land but never divides the people—each side simply minds its business.'

sentences.zh: 今以黃金二百斤賜將軍，便宜輒言。

translations.literal: Now with yellow gold two hundred jin bestow general; convenience then speak.
translations.idiomatic: We send two hundred jin of gold; say whatever further you need.'

sentences.zh: 』因授融為涼州牧。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon appointed Rong as Governor of Liang province.
translations.idiomatic: With that edict he named Dou Rong governor of Liang province.

sentences.zh: 璽書既至，河西咸驚，以為天子明見萬里之外，網羅張立之情。

translations.literal: When the imperial letter arrived, Hexi all were astonished, thought the Son of Heaven clearly saw beyond ten thousand li, the net-cord Zhang Li's situation.
translations.idiomatic: When the rescript reached Hexi, every officer marveled that the emperor saw events a thousand leagues away and understood how their alliance had formed.

sentences.zh: 融即復遣鈞上書曰：『臣融竊伏自惟，幸得托先後末屬，蒙恩為外戚，累世二千石。

translations.literal: Rong immediately again sent Jun submitting memorial saying: 'Your subject Rong humbly lies self reflecting, fortunately obtain trust former empress's minor kin, receive grace as outer kin, generations two thousand piculs.'
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong sent Liu Jun back with another memorial: 'Your servant humbly reflects that I have been fortunate to claim kinship with the late empress and to serve as affinal kin generation after generation at two thousand piculs.'

sentences.zh: 至臣之身，復備列位，假歷將帥，守持一隅。

translations.literal: Reaching your subject's body, again fill arranged ranks, borrowed experience generals commanders, guard hold one corner.
translations.idiomatic: In my own day I have risen through the ranks, held borrowed titles as general, and guarded a single corner of the realm.'

sentences.zh: 以委質則易為辭，以納忠則易為力。

translations.literal: By entrusting substance then easy to make phrases; by accepting loyalty then easy to make effort.
translations.idiomatic: Once I pledged myself, excuses came easily; now that I offer loyalty, action should come easily too.'

sentences.zh: 書不足以深達至誠，故遣劉鈞口陳肝膽。

translations.literal: Letter not sufficient deeply reach utmost sincerity, therefore send Liu Jun mouth state liver gall.
translations.idiomatic: Words on silk cannot carry the full depth of my sincerity; hence I sent Liu Jun to speak from the heart.'

sentences.zh: 自以底裏上露，長無纖介。

translations.literal: Self thought inner bottom upward exposed, long without fiber particle.
translations.idiomatic: I believed I had laid my inmost heart bare and that no slightest shadow would remain between us.'

sentences.zh: 而璽書盛稱蜀、漢二主，三分鼎足之權，任囂、尉佗之謀，竊自痛傷。

translations.literal: But the imperial letter greatly praised Shu and Han two lords, three divide tripod foot's power, Ren Xiao Wei Tuo's schemes—I privately pained wounded.
translations.idiomatic: Yet your rescript dwelled on the lords of Shu and Han, on tripod partitions and the schemes of Ren Xiao and Zhao Tuo—news that cut me to the heart.'

sentences.zh: 臣融雖無識，猶知利害之際，順逆之分。

translations.literal: Your subject Rong though without knowledge, still knows benefit harm's juncture, obedient rebellious's division.
translations.idiomatic: I am no sage, yet I know profit from ruin and loyalty from treason.'

sentences.zh: 豈可背真舊之主，事奸偽之人；

translations.literal: How can turn back true old's lord, serve treacherous false's persons;
translations.idiomatic: How could I abandon a legitimate sovereign to serve deceivers;

sentences.zh: 廢忠貞之節，為傾覆之事；

translations.literal: abolish loyal chaste's integrity, do overturning's affairs;
translations.idiomatic: discard steadfast honor for schemes that topple states;

sentences.zh: 棄已成之基，求無冀之利。

translations.literal: abandon already completed's foundation, seek without hope's profit.'
translations.idiomatic: or throw away a settled foundation to chase empty hopes?'

sentences.zh: 此三者雖問狂夫，猶知去就，而臣獨何以用心！

translations.literal: These three although ask mad man, still knows go come—yet your subject alone how use heart!
translations.idiomatic: Any madman asked these three things would know what to choose—why should I alone waver?'

sentences.zh: 謹遣同產弟友詣闕，口陳區區。

translations.literal: Respectfully send same-birth younger brother You reach palace gate, mouth state petty petty.'
translations.idiomatic: I therefore dispatch my full brother Dou You to the capital to speak my humble mind in person.'

sentences.zh: 』友至高平，會囂反叛，道絕，馳還，遣司馬席封間行通書。

translations.literal: ' When You reached Gaoping, met Xiao rebel revolt, roads cut, galloped back, sent marshal Xi Feng by-byway conduct letter.
translations.idiomatic: Dou You reached Gaoping just as Wei Xiao rebelled and the roads closed; he raced home and sent his marshal Xi Feng by secret paths to deliver letters.'

sentences.zh: 帝復遣席封賜融、友書，所以慰藉之甚備。

translations.literal: The emperor again sent Xi Feng bestow on Rong, You letters; the means comforting them very complete.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor sent Xi Feng back with letters for Dou Rong and Dou You, packed with reassurance.'

sentences.zh: 融既深知帝意，乃與隗囂書責讓之曰：

translations.literal: Rong having deeply known the emperor's intent, then with Wei Xiao letter blame reproach him saying:
translations.idiomatic: Once Dou Rong grasped the emperor's intent, he drafted a letter to Wei Xiao full of reproach:

sentences.zh: 伏惟將軍國富政修，士兵懷附。

translations.literal: I bow and think: general state rich government cultivated, officers soldiers cherish attach.
translations.idiomatic: I reflect that your territory is wealthy, your government well ordered, and your soldiers devoted to you.'

sentences.zh: 親遇厄會之際，國家不利之時，守節不回，承事本朝，後遣伯春委身於國，無疑之誠，於斯有效。

translations.literal: Personally met crisis conjunction's moment, state nation unlucky's time, guarded integrity not turn back, received served our dynasty; afterward sent Bochun entrust body to state—without doubt's sincerity—at this had effect.
translations.idiomatic: You stood firm when fate turned against the Han, kept faith with the legitimate court, and later sent your son Bochun to the capital as hostage—proof then and there of unquestioned loyalty.'

sentences.zh: 融等所以欣服高義，願從役於將軍者，良為此也。

translations.literal: Rong and others' reason admire submit high righteousness, wish follow service at general—truly is this.
translations.idiomatic: That is precisely why we admired your noble conduct and wished to serve under your banner.'

sentences.zh: 而忿悁之閒，改節易圖，君臣分爭，上下接兵。

translations.literal: But in anger resentment's interval, changed integrity altered plan, lord minister divided contend, above below join arms.
translations.idiomatic: Yet in a fit of pique you reversed course, sundering lord from minister and pitting high against low in arms.'

sentences.zh: 委成功，造難就，去從義，為橫謀，百年累之，一朝毀之，豈不惜乎！

translations.literal: Abandon completed merit, make difficulty succeed, leave follow righteousness, do horizontal scheme—a hundred years piled it, one morning destroyed it—how not pitiable!
translations.idiomatic: You cast aside victory to embrace disaster, leave righteousness for reckless intrigue. Generations of effort ruined in a morning—can you not regret it?'

sentences.zh: 殆執事者貪功建謀，以至於此，融竊痛之！

translations.literal: Perhaps those-in-charge greedy merit establish schemes, to reach to this—Rong privately pains it!
translations.idiomatic: Perhaps your advisers, greedy for glory, led you here; I grieve for it in private.'

sentences.zh: 當今西周地勢局迫，人兵離散，易以輔人，難以自建。

translations.literal: Now western Zhou terrain situation cramped, people troops scattered apart—easy thereby aid others, hard thereby self-build.
translations.idiomatic: Your western stronghold is cramped, your soldiers scattered: easy for you to aid another power, hard to stand alone.'

sentences.zh: 計若失路不反，聞道猶迷，不南合子陽，則北入文伯耳。

translations.literal: If plan loses road not return, hears way still lost—if not south unite Ziyang, then north enter Wenbo ear.
translations.idiomatic: If you stay lost and refuse the road back—if you spurn Gongsun Shu to the south you will only bolt north toward Lu Fang.'

sentences.zh: 夫負虛交而易強禦，恃遠救而輕近敵，未見其利也。

translations.literal: Those who bear empty alliances and treat lightly strong defense, rely distant rescue and slight near enemy—not seen their profit.
translations.idiomatic: Hollow alliances against a strong foe, distant rescue belittling a neighbor—nothing profit comes of it.'

sentences.zh: 融聞智者不危眾以舉事，仁者不違義以要功。

translations.literal: Rong hears wise ones do not endanger masses to raise affair; humane ones do not violate righteousness to demand merit.
translations.idiomatic: I have heard that the wise do not hazard their people on a whim, nor do the humane break duty for gain.'

sentences.zh: 今以小敵大，於眾何如？

translations.literal: Now with small oppose great—to the masses how?
translations.idiomatic: What will your followers make of pitting the weak against the strong?'

sentences.zh: 棄子僥功，於義何如？

translations.literal: Abandon son seek merit—to righteousness how?
translations.idiomatic: What virtue lies in abandoning a son to gamble on victory?'

sentences.zh: 且初事本朝，稽首北面，忠臣節也。

translations.literal: Moreover at first served our dynasty, knocked head north face—loyal minister's integrity.
translations.idiomatic: You first kowtowed toward the northern court—there was a loyal minister.'

sentences.zh: 及遣伯春，垂涕相送，慈父恩也。

translations.literal: When sent Bochun, tears hanging sent off—kind father's grace.
translations.idiomatic: You sent Bochun away with tears—a loving father's care.'

sentences.zh: 俄而背之，謂吏士何？

translations.literal: Soon after turned back on it—to officers soldiers what?
translations.idiomatic: Soon you betrayed that trust—what are your officers to think?'

sentences.zh: 忍而棄之，謂留子何？

translations.literal: Cruelly abandon him—to left-behind son what?
translations.idiomatic: You hardened your heart against him—what of the son you left behind?'

sentences.zh: 自兵起以來，轉相攻擊，城郭皆為丘墟，生人轉於溝壑。

translations.literal: Since weapons rose, turn mutual attack strike; city walls all became mound ruins; living people turn in ditches gullies.
translations.idiomatic: Since the wars began, blow has followed blow; cities lie in rubble and the living stumble into ditches and gullies.'

sentences.zh: 今其存者，非鋒刃之餘，則流亡之孤。

translations.literal: Now those who survive, if not blade edge's remainder, then wandering exile's orphans.
translations.idiomatic: Those who remain are either blade-scarred survivors or orphaned refugees.'

sentences.zh: 迄今傷痍之體未愈，哭泣之聲尚聞。

translations.literal: Until now wound injury's body not healed; weeping cry's sound still heard.
translations.idiomatic: Wounds have not yet closed; one still hears the sound of weeping.'

sentences.zh: 幸賴天運少還，而將軍復重於難，是使積屙不得遂瘳，幼孤將復流離，其為悲痛，尤足湣傷，言之可為酸鼻！

translations.literal: Fortunately rely Heaven's fortune slightly return, yet general again double on disaster—this makes piled illness not obtain achieve heal; young orphans will again wander separate—their being grief pain, especially enough pity wound—speaking it can make sour nose!
translations.idiomatic: Heaven had begun to relent, yet you pile disaster upon disaster, leaving chronic ills uncured and children adrift again—the grief of it wrings the heart and stings the nose to speak of!'

sentences.zh: 庸人且猶不忍，況仁者乎？

translations.literal: Mediocre man moreover still not bear—how humane ones?
translations.idiomatic: A common man could hardly bear it; how much less a man of mercy?'

sentences.zh: 融聞為忠甚易，得宜實難。

translations.literal: Rong hears doing loyalty very easy, obtaining right truly hard.
translations.idiomatic: I am told loyalty is easy to profess but hard to time rightly.'

sentences.zh: 憂人大過，以德取怨，知且以言獲罪也。

translations.literal: Worrying others too greatly, by virtue fetch resentment—know moreover by words obtain crime.
translations.idiomatic: If concern for you earns blame, accept it as the price of plain speech.'

sentences.zh: 區區所獻，惟將軍省焉。

translations.literal: Petty petty what offer—only general examine it.'
translations.idiomatic: I humbly submit these words for your consideration.'

sentences.zh: 囂不納。

translations.literal: Xiao did not accept.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao refused to listen.

sentences.zh: 融乃與五郡太守共砥厲兵馬，上疏請師期。

translations.literal: Rong then with the five commandery governors together sharpened troops and horses, submitted memorial requesting army rendezvous date.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong then joined the five commandery governors in honing their forces and submitted a memorial asking when imperial troops would march.

sentences.zh: 帝深嘉美之，乃賜融以外屬圖及太史公《五宗》、《外戚世家》、《魏其侯列傳》。

translations.literal: The emperor deeply praised it; then bestowed on Rong the chart of outer kin and Grand Scribe's 'Five Clans,' 'Hereditary Houses of Outer Kin,' and 'Hereditary House of Marquis Weiqi.'
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was deeply pleased and gave Dou Rong the genealogical chart of the imperial affines together with Sima Qian's chapters on the five imperial houses, the houses of the empresses' kin, and Marquis Weiqi.

sentences.zh: 詔報曰：『每追念外屬，孝景皇帝出自竇氏，定王，景帝之子，朕之所祖。

translations.literal: Edict replied saying: 'Each time I recall the outer kin—Emperor Xiaojing came from the Dou, King Ding of Liu was Emperor Jing's son, my ancestor.'
translations.idiomatic: The edict read in part: 'Whenever I think of the affinal families: Emperor Jing sprang from the Dou line; King Ding of Changshan was Emperor Jing's son—he is the ancestor I claim.'

sentences.zh: 昔魏其一言，繼統以正，長君、少君尊奉師傅，修成淑德，施及子孫，此皇太后神靈，上天祐漢也。

translations.literal: Formerly Weiqi with one word, inheritance succession by rectitude; Elder Lady and Younger Lady honored tutor teacher, cultivated accomplished virtue, extended to grandsons—this is Empress Dowager's spirit, Heaven aiding Han.'
translations.idiomatic: 'Once Marquis Weiqi spoke a single word and the rightful succession was secured; the elder and younger Dou ladies revered their teachers and cultivated virtue that reached their descendants—the dowager's spirit and Heaven's favor rest on the Han.'

sentences.zh: 從天水來者寫將軍所讓隗囂書，痛入骨髓。

translations.literal: Those who came from Tianshui copied out the general's letter reproaching Wei Xiao—it pierced to marrow bone.
translations.idiomatic: Travelers from Tianshui brought copies of your rebuke to Wei Xiao; those who read it felt it in their bones.

sentences.zh: 畔臣見之，當股栗慚愧，忠臣則酸鼻流涕，義士則曠若發目蒙，非忠孝愨誠，孰能如此？

translations.literal: Rebel ministers seeing it ought thigh-tremble ashamed; loyal ministers then sour-nose weep; righteous knights then vast as if hair-eye shade lifted—if not loyal filial sincere, who can thus?
translations.idiomatic: A traitor who saw it would tremble with shame; a loyal man would weep openly; a man of principle would feel a veil lifted from his eyes—only utter fidelity could produce such words.'

sentences.zh: 豈其德薄者所能克堪！

translations.literal: How can those of thin virtue be able to bear endure!
translations.idiomatic: Surely no petty man could have borne what you have done!'

sentences.zh: 囂自知失河西之助，族禍將及，欲設間離之說，亂惑真心，轉相解構，以成其奸。

translations.literal: Xiao self-knew he lost Hexi's aid; clan disaster about to reach; wished set estrangement-dividing persuasion, confuse delude true hearts, turn mutual unravel-structure, to complete his treachery.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao knew he had lost the west and faced ruin; he spread talk meant to drive a wedge between us, to cloud your judgment and stitch together slanders until his plot held.'

sentences.zh: 又京師百僚，不曉國家及將軍本意，多能采取虛偽，誇誕妄談，令忠孝失望，傳言乖實。

translations.literal: Also capital hundred officials, not understand state and general's original intent, many able adopt empty false, boast absurd wild talk, make loyal filial disappointed, transmitted words deviate from fact.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, many Luoyang officers do not grasp your aims or mine; they retail idle gossip and hollow boasts until loyal hearts despair and rumor drifts far from the truth.'

sentences.zh: 毀譽之來，皆不徒然，不可不思。

translations.literal: Destroy praise's coming, all not vain-sudden—cannot not think.
translations.idiomatic: Praise and blame never arrive without cause—think on that.'

sentences.zh: 今關東盜賊已定，大兵今當悉西，將軍其抗厲威武，以應期會。

translations.literal: Now east-of-pass bandits already settled; great army now ought all west; general you resist fierce martial might, to answer period rendezvous.'
translations.idiomatic: The bandits east of the passes are crushed; the main army will soon march west. Raise your banners and stand ready for the day we join forces.'

sentences.zh: 』融被詔，即與諸郡守將兵入金城。

translations.literal: ' Rong received edict; immediately with the commandery guards led troops into Jincheng.
translations.idiomatic: When the edict reached him, Dou Rong led the commandery forces into Jincheng at once.

sentences.zh: 初，更始時，先零羌封何諸種殺金城太守，居其郡，隗囂使使賂遺封何，與共結盟，欲發其眾。

translations.literal: Initially, in Gengshi's time, Xianling Qiang Feng He various kinds killed Jincheng governor, dwelt their commandery; Wei Xiao sent envoys bribing-gifting Feng He, with together knot alliance, wished deploy their crowds.
translations.idiomatic: Earlier, under Gengshi, Feng He of the Xianling Qiang had killed the governor of Jincheng and occupied the commandery. Wei Xiao sent envoys laden with gifts to win Feng He into alliance and planned to mobilize his warriors.

sentences.zh: 融等因軍出，進擊封何，大破之，斬首千餘級，得牛、馬、羊萬頭，谷數萬斛，因並河揚威武，伺候車駕。

translations.literal: Rong and others by army going out, advanced struck Feng He, greatly broke them, beheaded over thousand heads, obtained oxen horses sheep ten thousand head, grain several ten thousand hu; thereby along river raised martial might, awaited imperial carriage.
translations.idiomatic: As Dou Rong's army advanced he attacked Feng He, routed him, took more than a thousand heads, and captured ten thousand head of livestock and tens of thousands of hu of grain. He then paraded his might along the Yellow River while awaiting the emperor's arrival.

sentences.zh: 時，大兵未進，融乃引還。

translations.literal: At that time, great army not yet advanced; Rong then led back.
translations.idiomatic: The main host had not yet moved forward, so Dou Rong withdrew.

sentences.zh: 帝以融信效著明，益嘉之。

translations.literal: The emperor because Rong's trust effect manifest bright, increased praised him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor, seeing Dou Rong's loyalty proved beyond doubt, praised him all the more.

sentences.zh: 詔右扶風修理融父墳塋，祠以太牢。

translations.literal: Edict ordered Right Fufeng repair Rong father's tomb grave, sacrifice with grand beast.
translations.idiomatic: He ordered the governor of Right Fufeng to restore Dou Rong's father's tomb and to sacrifice a full ox, sheep, and pig there.

sentences.zh: 數馳輕使，致遺四方珍羞，梁統乃使人刺殺張玄，絕與囂絕，皆解所假將軍印綬。

translations.literal: Repeatedly dispatched swift envoys bearing gifts and delicacies from every quarter; Liang Tong then sent men to assassinate Zhang Xuan and thereby broke with Wei Xiao—all returned their borrowed general's seals and cords.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Guangwu sent fast couriers bearing rare delicacies from across the realm. Liang Tong had Zhang Xuan murdered and severed ties with Wei Xiao; each commander surrendered the borrowed general's commission.

sentences.zh: 七年夏，酒泉太守竺曾以弟報怨殺人而去郡，融承制拜曾為武鋒將軍，更以辛肜代之。

translations.literal: Seventh year summer, Jiuquan governor Zhu Zeng because younger brother avenge hate kill person left commandery; Rong receiving mandate appointed Zeng as Martial Vanguard general, replaced with Xin Ran in his place.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the seventh Jianwu year the governor of Jiuquan, Zhu Zeng, fled his post after his brother killed a man in a private feud. Acting under imperial authority Dou Rong named Zhu Zeng General of the Martial Vanguard and replaced him at Jiuquan with Xin Ran.

sentences.zh: 秋，隗囂發兵寇安定，帝將自西征之，先戒融期。

translations.literal: Autumn, Wei Xiao deployed troops raid Anding; emperor will personally west campaign him; first warned Rong the date.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Wei Xiao struck Anding. The emperor meant to lead the expedition west himself and sent Dou Rong a preliminary timetable.

sentences.zh: 會遇雨，道斷，且囂兵已退，乃止。

translations.literal: Met encountering rain, roads cut; moreover Xiao troops already retreated—thereupon stopped.
translations.idiomatic: Heavy rain closed the roads, and Wei Xiao had already pulled back, so the campaign halted.

sentences.zh: 融至姑臧，被詔罷歸。

translations.literal: Rong reached Guzang, received edict dismiss return.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong reached Guzang only to receive orders dismissing him to his command.

sentences.zh: 融恐大兵遂久不出，乃上書曰：『隗囂聞車駕當西，臣融東下，士眾騷動，計且不戰。

translations.literal: Rong feared great army then long not emerge; then submitted memorial saying: 'Wei Xiao heard imperial carriage will west, your subject Rong east down—troops masses restless—plan likely not fight.'
translations.idiomatic: Fearing that the imperial army might delay too long, Dou Rong wrote: 'When Wei Xiao hears that Your Majesty is marching west while your servant turns east, his army will panic and may refuse to give battle.'

sentences.zh: 囂將高峻之屬皆欲逢迎大軍，後聞兵罷，峻等復疑。

translations.literal: Xiao's generals Gao Jun and sorts all wish meet welcome great army; afterward heard troops dismissed—Jun and others again doubted.
translations.idiomatic: Generals such as Gao Jun were ready to welcome your advance; when they learned the campaign had been called off, they wavered again.

sentences.zh: 囂揚言東方有變，西州豪傑遂復附從。

translations.literal: Xiao raised words east had change; western province heroes then again attached followed.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Xiao spread rumors of unrest in the east, and the magnates of the western provinces rallied to him once more.

sentences.zh: 囂又引公孫述將，令守突門。

translations.literal: Xiao again led Gongsun Shu's generals, ordered guard burst gates.
translations.idiomatic: He even brought in officers from Gongsun Shu to hold the outer gates.

sentences.zh: 臣融孤弱，介在其間，雖承威靈，宜速救助。

translations.literal: Your subject Rong isolated weak, stuck between them—although receiving awesome spirit, ought speedily rescue aid.'
translations.idiomatic: Your servant stands alone between those fires; though I lean on your prestige I need relief without delay.'

sentences.zh: 國家當其前，臣融促其後，緩急叠用，首尾相資，囂勢排迮，不得進退，此必破也。

translations.literal: State faces him front, your subject Rong urges him rear—urgent-leisure pile use—head tail mutually aid—Xiao's situation squeezed narrow—cannot advance retreat—this surely broken.'
translations.idiomatic: Strike him from the front while I press him from the rear; alternate swift blows with steady pressure so he cannot move—boxed in, he must break.'

sentences.zh: 若兵不早進，久生持疑，則外長寇仇，內示困弱，復令讒邪得有因緣，臣竊憂之。

translations.literal: If troops not early advance, long born hold doubt—then outward lengthen foe enemies, inward show trapped weak; again order slander evil obtain causes—your subject privately worries it.'
translations.idiomatic: If the army tarries, doubt will grow abroad we embolden the enemy; at home we look feeble and give malice an opening—I tremble at the thought.'

sentences.zh: 惟陛下哀憐！

translations.literal: Only Your Majesty pity sorrow!
translations.idiomatic: I beg Your Majesty's mercy!'

sentences.zh: 』帝深美之。

translations.literal: ' Emperor deeply praised it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor admired the memorial.

sentences.zh: 八年夏，車駕西征隗囂，融率五郡太守及羌虜小月氏等步騎數萬，輜重五千餘兩，與大軍會高平第一。

translations.literal: Eighth year summer, imperial carriage west campaigned Wei Xiao; Rong led five commandery governors and Qiang foreigners Lesser Yuezhi etc. foot cavalry several tens of thousands, baggage trains over five thousand vehicles, with great army met Gaoping first.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the eighth year Guangwu marched west against Wei Xiao. Dou Rong brought the five commandery governors, Qiang and Lesser Yuezhi auxiliaries numbering tens of thousands of horse and foot, and more than five thousand supply carts to join the main army at the first camp at Gaoping.

sentences.zh: 融先遣從事問會見儀適。

translations.literal: Rong first sent attendant asked meeting see etiquette propriety.
translations.idiomatic: Before the audience Dou Rong sent an aide to inquire after court etiquette.

sentences.zh: 是時，軍旅代興，諸將與三公交錯道中，或背使者交私語。

translations.literal: At this time, armies successive rise; various generals with three dukes crossed paths in road—some turned back envoys whispered private words.
translations.idiomatic: Armies were everywhere; generals and the Three Excellencies crossed paths on the roads, some turning their backs to whisper.

sentences.zh: 帝聞融先問禮儀，甚善之，以宣告百僚。

translations.literal: Emperor heard Rong first asked ritual etiquette, very good it; thereby proclaimed announce hundred officials.
translations.idiomatic: When the emperor heard that Dou Rong had asked about ritual first, he approved and announced it to the full bureaucracy.

sentences.zh: 乃置酒高會，引見融等，待以殊禮。

translations.literal: Thereupon set wine high feast, led see Rong etc., treated with special ritual.
translations.idiomatic: He then held a banquet and received Dou Rong with honors reserved for the foremost servants of the throne.

sentences.zh: 拜弟友為奉車都尉，從弟士太中大夫。

translations.literal: Appointed younger brother You as Bearer of the Vitara for the Heir Apparent; younger cousin Shi as Grand Palace Gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: He named Dou You Bearer of the Vitara for the Heir Apparent and Dou Shi, his younger cousin, Grand Palace Gentleman.

sentences.zh: 遂共進軍，囂眾大潰，城邑皆降。

translations.literal: Thereupon jointly advanced army; Xiao's crowds greatly routed; cities all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: They advanced together; Wei Xiao's host collapsed and city after city capitulated.

sentences.zh: 帝高融功，下詔以安豐、陽泉、蓼、安風四縣封融為安豐侯，弟友為顯親侯。

translations.literal: The emperor lauding Rong's merit issued an edict enfeoffing Rong as marquis of Anfeng with revenue from the four counties Anfeng, Yangquan, Liao, and Anfeng (homophonous county written with 'wind'); younger brother You as marquis of Xianqin.
translations.idiomatic: Honoring Dou Rong's achievement, the emperor made him marquis of Anfeng with the four supporting counties (Anfeng, Yangquan, Liao, and Anfeng written with the graph for 'wind') and named Dou You marquis of Xianqin.

sentences.zh: 遂以次封諸將帥：武鋒將軍竺曾為助義侯，武威太守梁統為成義侯，張掖太守史苞為褒義侯，金城太守厙鈞為輔義侯，酒泉太守辛肜為扶義侯。

translations.literal: Thereupon by order enfeoffed various generals commanders: Martial Vanguard general Zhu Zhu as Marquis Who Aids Righteousness; Wuwei governor Liang Tong as Marquis Who Completes Righteousness; Zhangye governor Shi Bao as Marquis Who Praises Righteousness; Jincheng governor She Jun as Marquis Who Assists Righteousness; Jiuquan governor Xin Ran as Marquis Who Upholds Righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: The other commanders followed in turn: Zhu Zeng as marquis who aids righteousness, Liang Tong as marquis who completes righteousness, Shi Bao as marquis who commends righteousness, She Jun as marquis who assists righteousness, and Xin Ran as marquis who upholds righteousness.

sentences.zh: 封爵既畢，乘輿東歸，悉遣融等西還所鎮。

translations.literal: Enfeoffments already finished, imperial carriage east returned; all sent Rong etc. west return what they garrisoned.
translations.idiomatic: Once the titles were granted the emperor turned east and sent Dou Rong and the others back to their western posts.

sentences.zh: 融以兄弟並受爵位，久專方面，懼不自安，數上書求代。

translations.literal: Rong because brothers together received noble ranks, long monopolized regions—feared not self secure; repeatedly submitted memorials seeking replacement.
translations.idiomatic: Because he and his brothers now held high noble rank and had commanded their frontier for years, Dou Rong grew uneasy and repeatedly asked to be relieved.

sentences.zh: 詔報曰：『吾與將軍如左右手耳，數執謙退，何不曉人意？

translations.literal: Edict replied saying: 'I with general like left right hand—many times hold modest retreat—how not understand men's intent?
translations.idiomatic: The reply read: 'You and I are like two hands on one body; why do you keep refusing office—do you think I cannot read a man's heart?'

sentences.zh: 勉循士民，無擅離部曲。』

translations.literal: Strive follow scholars commoners—do not dare leave troops without authority.'
translations.idiomatic: Stay with your people and do not abandon your army without leave.'

sentences.zh: 及隴、蜀平，詔融與五郡太守奏事京師，官屬賓客相隨，駕乘千餘兩，馬、牛、羊被野。

translations.literal: When Long and Shu pacified, edict Rong with five commandery governors report affairs capital; official retainers guests followed; carriages rides over thousand vehicles; horses oxen sheep cover wild.
translations.idiomatic: After Longxi and Ba–Shu were pacified Dou Rong and the five governors were summoned to report at the capital. Officials, retainers, and clients filled more than a thousand wagons; herds of livestock stretched across the fields.

sentences.zh: 融到，詣洛陽城門，上涼州牧、張掖屬國都尉、安豐侯印綬，詔遣使者還侯印綬。

translations.literal: Rong arrived, went to Luoyang city gate, submitted Governor of Liang province, Zhangye dependent state commandant, Marquis of Anfeng seals cords; edict sent envoy return marquis seals cords.
translations.idiomatic: At the Luoyang gate Dou Rong surrendered the seals of governor of Liang, commandant of Zhangye dependent state, and marquis of Anfeng; the court ordered them returned at once.

sentences.zh: 引見，就諸侯位，賞賜恩寵，傾動京師。

translations.literal: Led audience, took feudal lords' seats; rewards grants favor grace—shook capital.
translations.idiomatic: He was led to audience and seated among the feudal lords; gifts and honors dazzled the capital.

sentences.zh: 數月，拜為冀州牧，十餘日，又遷大司空。

translations.literal: Several months, appointed as Ji province governor; over ten days, again promoted Grand Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Within months he was named governor of Ji province; within ten days more he rose to Grand Minister of Works.

sentences.zh: 融自以非舊臣，一旦入朝，在功臣之右，每召會進見，容貌辭氣卑恭已甚，帝以此愈親厚之。

translations.literal: Rong self thought not old minister; once entered court; among merit ministers' right—each summoned meeting advance audience—appearance bearing speech breath humble respectful already extreme—emperor by this more intimate thick him.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong knew he was no veteran of the founding; suddenly he stood above the battle-hardened generals. Whenever summoned he appeared almost timid in bearing and speech, and the emperor drew closer to him for it.

sentences.zh: 融小心，久不自安，數辭讓爵位，因侍中金遷口達至誠。

translations.literal: Rong careful; long not self secure; several times declined yielded noble ranks; through attendant Jin Qian mouth expressed utmost sincerity.
translations.idiomatic: Still anxious, he repeatedly tried to surrender his titles and spoke his deepest feelings through the palace attendant Jin Qian.

sentences.zh: 又上疏曰：『臣融年五十三。

translations.literal: Also submitted memorial saying: 'Your subject Rong age fifty-three.'
translations.idiomatic: He wrote again: 'Your servant is fifty-three.'

sentences.zh: 有子年十五，質性頑鈍。

translations.literal: Have son age fifteen; nature disposition dull stubborn.
translations.idiomatic: 'My son is fifteen and slow-witted.'

sentences.zh: 臣融朝夕教導以經藝，不得令觀天文，見讖記。

translations.literal: Your subject Rong dawn dusk instruct guide with classics arts—cannot order observe astronomy, see prophecy records.
translations.idiomatic: I drill him daily in the classics and forbid him astrology or apocrypha.'

sentences.zh: 誠欲令恭肅畏事，恂恂循道，不願其有才能，何況乃當傳以連城廣土，享故諸侯王國哉？

translations.literal: Truly wish order respectful solemn fear affairs, cautious cautious follow way—not wish him have talent ability—how much more then ought transmit linked cities wide soil, enjoy former feudal kings' states?
translations.idiomatic: I want him grave, cautious, and obedient, not clever; how could he inherit broad domains like the old kings of the feudal era?'

sentences.zh: 』因復請間求見，帝不許。

translations.literal: ' Thereupon again requested interval seek audience; emperor not permitted.
translations.idiomatic: He asked for a private audience; the emperor refused.

sentences.zh: 後朝罷，逡巡席後，帝知欲有讓，遂使左右傳出。

translations.literal: After court dismissed, hesitated hesitate behind seats; emperor knew wished to have yield—then ordered attendants convey him out.
translations.idiomatic: After court he hung back until the emperor, guessing he meant to demur, had attendants escort him out.

sentences.zh: 它日會見，迎詔融曰：『日者知公欲讓職還土，故命公暑熱且自便。

translations.literal: Another day met audience, welcomed edict to Rong saying: 'Other day knew duke wished yield office return soil—therefore ordered duke summer heat and self convenience.'
translations.idiomatic: At their next meeting Guangwu cut him off: 'The other day I knew you wanted to give up your office and fief, so I let you escape the summer heat.'

sentences.zh: 今相見，宜論它事，勿得復言。

translations.literal: Now meet see—ought discuss other matters—do not obtain again speak.'
translations.idiomatic: Now we meet—speak of anything else but that.'

sentences.zh: 』融不敢重陳請。

translations.literal: ' Rong not dare again repeat request.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong dared not raise the matter again.

sentences.zh: 二十年，大司徒戴涉坐所舉人盜金下獄，帝以三公參職，不得已乃策免融。

translations.literal: Twentieth year, Grand Minister of Education Dai She sit recommended person steal gold down prison; emperor because Three Excellencies joint duty—had no choice then document dismiss Rong.
translations.idiomatic: In the twentieth year Grand Minister of Education Dai She was imprisoned because a man he had recommended stole gold. Because the Three Excellencies shared responsibility, the emperor reluctantly dismissed Dou Rong by edict.

sentences.zh: 明年，加位特進。

translations.literal: Next year, added rank specially advanced.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he received the honorary rank of Specially Advanced.

sentences.zh: 二十三年，代陰興行衛尉事，特進如故，又兼領將作大匠。

translations.literal: Twenty-third year, replaced Yin Xing conducting Commandant of the Guards affairs; specially advanced as before; also concurrently led Grand Adjunct of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-third year he took Yin Xing's place as acting Commandant of the Guards while keeping his Specially Advanced standing and added the Grand Adjunct of Works.

sentences.zh: 弟友為城門校尉，兄弟並典禁兵。

translations.literal: Younger brother You as Colonel of the City Gates; brothers together managed forbidden troops.
translations.idiomatic: His brother Dou You became Colonel of the City Gates; the two brothers together commanded the palace guard.

sentences.zh: 融復乞骸骨，輒賜錢、帛，太官致珍奇。

translations.literal: Rong again begged old bones; always bestowed cash silk; Grand Provisioner sent rare delicacies.
translations.idiomatic: Each time Dou Rong asked to retire the emperor sent cash and silk and the imperial kitchen sent rare dishes.

sentences.zh: 及友卒，帝湣融年衰，遣中常侍、中謁者即其臥內強進酒食。

translations.literal: When You died, emperor pitied Rong's age decline—sent palace attendant and palace usher reach his bedchamber force advance wine food.
translations.idiomatic: After Dou You died the emperor, pitying Dou Rong's age, sent eunuchs and ushers to his bedside to press food and drink on him.

sentences.zh: 融長子穆，尚內黃公主，代友為城門校尉。

translations.literal: Rong eldest son Mu, married Neihuang princess—replaced You as Colonel of the City Gates.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong's eldest son Dou Mu married the Princess of Neihuang and succeeded Dou You as colonel of the gates.

sentences.zh: 穆子勛，尚東海恭王彊女沘陽公主，友子固，亦尚光武女涅陽公主。

translations.literal: Mu's son Xun married Prince Gong of East Sea Qiang's daughter Princess Biyang; You's son Gu also married Guangwu's daughter Princess Neyang.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Mu's son Dou Xun married the Princess of Biyang, daughter of Prince Gong of the East Sea, Liu Qiang; Dou You's son Dou Gu married Emperor Guangwu's daughter the Princess of Neyang.

sentences.zh: 顯宗即位，以融從兄子林為護羌校尉，竇氏一公、兩侯、三公主、四二千石，相與並時。

translations.literal: Emperor Ming ascended; because Rong's elder cousin's son Lin as Colonel Who Protects the Qiang—Dou clan one duke, two marquises, three princesses, four two-thousand piculs—mutually together same time.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Ming came to the throne he named Dou Lin, Dou Rong's elder cousin's son, Colonel Who Protects the Qiang. The Dou family counted one duke, two marquises, three imperial princesses, and four officials at two thousand piculs—all at once.

sentences.zh: 自祖及孫，官府邸第相望京邑，奴婢以千數，於親戚、功臣中莫與為此。

translations.literal: From ancestor to grandson—official offices mansions look toward each other capital city—slave maidservants by thousand count—among kin merit ministers none with him did this.
translations.idiomatic: From grandfather to grandsons their mansions lined the capital and they owned thousands of slaves; no other affinal or merit house matched them.

sentences.zh: 永平二年，林以罪誅，事在《西羌傳》。

translations.literal: Yongping second year, Lin by crime executed—the affair in 'Western Qiang Treatise.'
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Yongping Dou Lin was executed for his crimes; the case is told in the Treatise on the Western Qiang.

sentences.zh: 帝由是數下詔切責融，戒以竇嬰、田蚡禍敗之事。

translations.literal: Emperor thereupon several times issued edicts sharply blame Rong—warn with Dou Ying Tian Fen disaster defeat affairs.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor then rebuked Dou Rong repeatedly by edict and warned him with the falls of Dou Ying and Tian Fen.

sentences.zh: 融惶恐乞骸骨，詔令歸第養病。

translations.literal: Rong fearful begged old bones—edict ordered return mansion nurture illness.
translations.idiomatic: Terror-stricken, Dou Rong asked to retire and was ordered home to convalesce.

sentences.zh: 歲餘，聽上衛尉印綬，賜養牛，上樽酒。

translations.literal: After year plus, permitted submit Commandant of the Guards seal cord—bestowed nurture ox—presented superior wine.
translations.idiomatic: A year later he was allowed to surrender the seals of Commandant of the Guards and received an ox for his sustenance and jars of fine wine.

sentences.zh: 融在宿衛十餘年，年老，子孫縱誕，多不法。

translations.literal: Rong in night guard over ten years—old age—grandsons sons vertical unrestrained—many not lawful.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Rong had stood night watch in the palace for more than a decade; in old age his sons and grandsons ran wild and broke the law often.

sentences.zh: 穆等遂交通輕薄，屬託郡縣，干亂政事。

translations.literal: Mu etc. then associated with frivolous—entrust county commanderies—interfere disorder government affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Mu and his circle cultivated worthless men, pulled strings in the counties, and meddled in government.

sentences.zh: 以封在安豐，欲令姻戚悉據故六安國，遂矯稱陰太后詔，令六安侯劉盱去婦，因以女妻之。

translations.literal: Because fief at Anfeng—wished order marriage kin all occupy former Lu'an kingdom—thereupon falsely claimed Empress Dowager Yin's edict—ordered Lu'an marquis Liu Xu dismiss wife—thereby with daughter marry him.
translations.idiomatic: Their fief lay in Anfeng; they wanted all their affines planted across old Lu'an and forged an edict of Empress Dowager Yin ordering Marquis Liu Xu of Lu'an to divorce his wife so they could marry him a Dou daughter.

sentences.zh: 五年，盱婦家上書言狀，帝大怒，乃盡免穆等官，諸竇為郎吏者皆將家屬歸故郡，獨留融京師。

translations.literal: Fifth year, Xu's wife's family submitted memorial stating situation—emperor greatly angry—then all dismissed Mu etc. offices—all Dou who were gentleman clerks led families return native commanderies—only left Rong capital.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year Liu Xu's former in-laws exposed the plot. The emperor dismissed Dou Mu and his associates and sent every Dou who held a minor post back to his home commandery, keeping only Dou Rong in the capital.

sentences.zh: 穆等西至函谷關，有詔悉復追還。

translations.literal: Mu etc. west reached Hangu Pass—there was edict all again pursue return.
translations.idiomatic: As Dou Mu's party reached Hangu Pass couriers recalled them all.

sentences.zh: 會融卒，時年七十八，謚曰戴侯，賻送甚厚。

translations.literal: Met Rong died—at time age seventy-eight—posthumous title said Dai Marquis—funeral gifts very thick.
translations.idiomatic: Before matters settled Dou Rong died at seventy-eight, posthumously titled Marquis Dai, with a lavish state funeral.

sentences.zh: 帝以穆不能修尚，而擁富資，居大第，常令謁者一人監護其家。

translations.literal: Emperor because Mu could not cultivate uphold—yet embrace rich resources—dwell great mansion—always ordered usher one person supervise guard his household.
translations.idiomatic: Because Dou Mu could not live modestly though immensely rich and housed in a vast mansion, the emperor assigned an usher to watch his household.

sentences.zh: 居數年，謁者奏穆父子自失勢，數出怨望語，帝令將家屬歸本郡，唯勛以沘陽主婿留京師。

translations.literal: Dwelling several years—usher memorialized Mu father-son self lost power—several uttered resentment words—emperor ordered lead families return native commandery—only Xun because Biyang princess husband remained capital.
translations.idiomatic: After some years the usher reported that Dou Mu and his son, stripped of influence, were muttering resentment; the emperor ordered the clan back to its commandery, leaving only Dou Xun in Luoyang as the princess's husband.

sentences.zh: 穆坐賂遺小吏，郡捕系，與子宣俱死平陵獄，勛亦死洛陽獄。

translations.literal: Mu, convicted for bribing a petty official, was seized by the commandery and bound—he and son Xuan together died in Pingling prison—Xun also died in Luoyang prison.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Mu was arrested for bribing a county clerk; he and his son Dou Xuan died in the Pingling jail. Dou Xun died as well in the Luoyang prison.

sentences.zh: 久之，詔還融夫人與小孫一人居洛陽家舍。

translations.literal: Long after, edict return Rong's wife with one small grandson dwell Luoyang house.
translations.idiomatic: Much later an edict allowed Dou Rong's widow and one young grandson to live again in the family's Luoyang house.

sentences.zh: 十四年，封勛弟嘉為安豐侯，食邑二千戶，奉融後。

translations.literal: Fourteenth year, enfeoffed Xun's younger brother Jia as Marquis of Anfeng—food fief two thousand households—serve Rong's posterity.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourteenth year Dou Jia, Dou Xun's younger brother, was made marquis of Anfeng with two thousand households to carry on Dou Rong's line.

sentences.zh: 和帝初，為少府。

translations.literal: Emperor He's beginning—served as Privy Treasurer.
translations.idiomatic: At the start of Emperor He's reign he served as Privy Treasurer.

sentences.zh: 及勛子大將軍憲被誅，免就國。

translations.literal: When Xun's son Grand General Xian was executed—dismissed went to kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Xian, Dou Xun's son and Grand General, was executed, Jia was stripped of office and sent to his fief.

sentences.zh: 嘉卒，子萬全嗣。

translations.literal: Jia died—son Wanquan succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Jia died and was succeeded by his son Dou Wanquan.

sentences.zh: 萬全卒，子會宗嗣。

translations.literal: Wanquan died—son Huizong succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Wanquan died and was succeeded by his son Dou Huizong.

sentences.zh: 萬全弟子武，別有傳。

translations.literal: Wanquan's younger brother's son Wu—separately has biography.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Wu, son of Dou Wanquan's younger brother, has his own biography.

sentences.zh: 論曰：竇融始以豪俠為名，拔起風塵之中，以投天隙。

translations.literal: Discourse says: Dou Rong began with heroic knight reputation—uprooted rose wind dust within—to cast into Heaven's crack.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: Dou Rong first made his name as a bold knight, rose from the turmoil of the age, and seized the opening Heaven offered.

sentences.zh: 遂蟬蛻王侯之尊，終膺卿相之位，此則僥功趣勢之士也。

translations.literal: Thereupon cicada-molted kings marquises' honor—finally bore minister duke's position—this then seeks merit hurries power's knights.
translations.idiomatic: He shed the mantle of regional lord and ended as minister of state—such are men who chase opportunity.

sentences.zh: 及其爵位崇滿，至乃放遠權寵，恂恂似若不能已者，又何智也！

translations.literal: When his noble rank high full—to extent released distant power favor—cautious cautious seeming if not able stop—again what wisdom!
translations.idiomatic: Yet when his titles peaked he drew back from power and clung to humility as if he could not stop—what good sense was that!

sentences.zh: 嘗獨詳味此子之風度，雖經國之術無足多談，而進退之禮良可言矣。

translations.literal: Once alone detailed taste this man's bearing—although ordering state's arts not worth much discuss—but advance retreat's rites truly can be spoken.
translations.idiomatic: Contemplating his manner, one finds little to say about statecraft, but much to admire in how he knew when to advance and when to yield.

sentences.zh: 苗、護烏桓校尉文穆將太原、雁門、代郡、上谷、漁陽、右北平、定襄郡兵及烏桓、鮮卑萬一千騎出平城塞。

translations.literal: Miao, Colonel Who Protects the Wuhuan Wen Mu led Taiyuan, Yanmen, Dai, Shanggu, Yuyang, Youbeiping, Dingxiang commandery troops and Wuhuan, Xianbei eleven thousand riders out Pingcheng frontier pass.
translations.idiomatic: Lai Miao and Wen Mu, Colonel Who Protects the Wuhuan, led troops from Taiyuan, Yanmen, Dai, Shanggu, Yuyang, Youbeiping, and Dingxiang together with eleven thousand Wuhuan and Xianbei horsemen out of Pingcheng Pass.

sentences.zh: 固、忠至天山，擊呼衍王，斬首千餘級。

translations.literal: Gu, Zhong reached Heavenly Mountain—struck Huyan king—beheaded over thousand heads.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Gu and Zhi reached the Heavenly Mountains and attacked the Huyan king, taking more than a thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 呼衍王走，追至蒲類海。

translations.literal: Huyan king fled—pursued reached Lake Pulei.
translations.idiomatic: The Huyan king fled; they chased him to Lake Barkul.

sentences.zh: 留吏士屯伊吾盧城。

translations.literal: Left officers soldiers garrison Yiwulu city.
translations.idiomatic: They left troops to hold Yiwulu.

sentences.zh: 耿秉、秦彭絕漠六百餘里，至三木樓山，來苗、文穆至匈奴河水上，虜皆奔走，無所獲。

translations.literal: Geng Bing, Qin Peng cut desert six hundred plus li—reached Sanmulou mountain—Lai Miao, Wen Mu reached Xiongnu river water on—the prisoners all ran—nothing obtained.
translations.idiomatic: Geng Bing and Qin Peng crossed six hundred li of desert to Mount Sanmulou; Lai Miao and Wen Mu reached the Xiongnu River. The enemy fled and they took no prisoners.

sentences.zh: 祭肜、吳棠坐不至涿邪山，免為庶人。

translations.literal: Zhai Rong and Wu Tang, convicted for not reaching Zhuoye Mountain—dismissed and made commoners.
translations.idiomatic: Zhai Rong and Wu Tang lost their titles for failing to reach Mount Zhuoye and were reduced to commoners.

sentences.zh: 時，諸將唯固有功，加位特進。

translations.literal: At the time, among the various generals only Gu had merit; his rank was raised to Specially Advanced.
translations.idiomatic: Of all the generals only Dou Gu earned distinction and was promoted to the honorary rank of Specially Advanced.

sentences.zh: 明年，復出玉門擊西域，詔耿秉及騎都尉劉張皆去符傳以屬固。

translations.literal: The next year he again went out through the Jade Gate to campaign in the Western Regions; an edict ordered Geng Bing and Colonel of Equerry Liu Zhang both to yield their tallies and credentials and place themselves under Gu.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he marched again through the Jade Gate against the Western Regions. Emperor Zhang instructed Geng Bing and Colonel of Equerry Liu Zhang to hand over their credentials and serve under Dou Gu.

sentences.zh: 固遂破白山，降車師，事已具《耿秉傳》。

translations.literal: Gu thereupon broke White Mountain and submitted the chariot masters; the affair is fully recorded in the 'Biography of Geng Bing.'
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu took White Mountain and forced the king of the chariot people to yield; the rest is told in the biography of Geng Bing.

sentences.zh: 固在邊數年，羌胡服其恩信。

translations.literal: Gu was on the frontier several years; Qiang and Hu submitted to his kindness and trustworthiness.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu spent years on the frontier, and Qiang and Hu alike respected his benevolence and good faith.

sentences.zh: 固久歷大位，甚見尊貴，賞賜租祿，貲累巨億，而性謙儉，愛人好施，士以此稱之。

translations.literal: Gu long occupied high office, was greatly honored, rewards and stipends piled up—assets accumulating to hundreds of millions—yet his nature was humble and frugal; he loved others and delighted in giving; gentlemen praised him for this.
translations.idiomatic: He held high rank for a long time and amassed vast wealth from salaries and gifts, yet remained modest and generous; scholars esteemed him for it.

sentences.zh: 章和二年卒。

translations.literal: He died in the second year of Zhanghe.
translations.idiomatic: He died in the second year of the Zhanghe era (88 CE).

sentences.zh: 謚曰文侯。

translations.literal: Posthumous title: Marquis Wen.
translations.idiomatic: His posthumous title was Marquis Wen.

sentences.zh: 子彪，至射聲校尉，先固卒，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Son Biao reached Colonel of the Shooters at the Ready; he died before Gu, without sons—the marquisate was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: His son Dou Biao rose to colonel of the Shooters at the Ready but predeceased his father without heirs, so the fief lapsed.

sentences.zh: 憲字伯度。

translations.literal: Xian, courtesy name Bodu.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian, whose courtesy name was Bodu.

sentences.zh: 父勛被誅，憲少孤。

translations.literal: Father Xun was executed; Xian was orphaned young.
translations.idiomatic: His father Dou Xun had been put to death, leaving Dou Xian fatherless in childhood.

sentences.zh: 建初二年，女弟立為皇后，拜憲為郎，稍遷侍中、虎賁中郎將；

translations.literal: In the second year of Jianchu his younger sister was established as empress; Xian was appointed gentleman and gradually promoted to palace attendant and Colonel of the Gentlemen of the Household for All Purposes;
translations.idiomatic: In the second Jianchu year his sister became empress; Dou Xian received appointment as a gentleman, then rose to palace attendant and colonel of the elite imperial guard;

sentences.zh: 弟篤，為黃門侍郎。

translations.literal: younger brother Du, served as Gentleman at the Yellow Gates.
translations.idiomatic: his brother Dou Du became a gentleman at the Yellow Gates.

sentences.zh: 兄弟親幸，並侍宮省，賞賜累積，寵貴日盛，自王、主及陰、馬諸家，莫不畏憚。

translations.literal: The brothers were intimate favorites and together attended the inner palace; rewards accumulated and their favor daily flourished—from kings and princesses to the Yin and Ma houses none did not fear them.
translations.idiomatic: The brothers shared the inner chambers as favored kin; gifts mounted until even royal princes and the great Yin and Ma clans went in fear of them.

sentences.zh: 憲恃宮掖聲勢，遂以賤直請奪沁水公主園田，主逼畏，不敢計。

translations.literal: Xian relied on the palace's prestige and therefore requested to seize the Princess of Qinshui's garden fields at a contemptible price; the princess was compelled and afraid and dared not reckon.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian traded on court influence and tried to bully the Princess of Qinshui out of her orchards for a pittance; intimidated, she did not dare resist.

sentences.zh: 後肅宗駕出過園，指以向憲，憲陰喝不得對。

translations.literal: Later Emperor Zhang's carriage went out passing the garden and pointed it toward Xian; Xian secretly shouted so he could not answer.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Emperor Zhang drove past the estate and asked Dou Xian about it, Dou Xian silenced him with a glare so he could not reply.

sentences.zh: 後發覺，帝大怒，召憲切責曰：『深思前過，奪主田園過，何用愈趙高指鹿為馬？

translations.literal: Afterward it was discovered; the emperor greatly angry summoned Xian and sharply blamed saying: 'Deeply ponder former faults—seizing the princess's garden fields—what use surpasses Zhao Gao pointing deer as horse?'
translations.idiomatic: When the truth emerged the emperor summoned Dou Xian in fury. 'Think hard on your past crime,' he said. 'Robbing an imperial princess of her land—how is that better than Zhao Gao calling a deer a horse?'

sentences.zh: 久念使人驚怖。

translations.literal: Long pondering makes men tremble with terror.
translations.idiomatic: The longer one dwells on it, the more horrifying it becomes.'

sentences.zh: 昔永平中，常令陰黨、陰博、鄧疊三人更相糾察，故諸豪戚莫敢犯法者，而詔書切切，猶以舅氏田宅為言。

translations.literal: Formerly in Yongping times, often ordered Yin Dang, Yin Bo, Deng Die three men mutually investigate—therefore the various powerful kin dared not violate law; yet edicts were urgent, still taking maternal uncles' fields and houses as topic.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yongping the emperor had Yin Dang, Yin Bo, and Deng Die watch one another so that great affinal houses dared not break the law; edicts still harped on fields and mansions of the empress's kin.'

sentences.zh: 今貴主尚見枉奪，何況小人哉！

translations.literal: Now honored princesses still see wrongful seizure—how much more petty men!
translations.idiomatic: Even exalted princesses suffer seizure of their property—what hope for commoners?'

sentences.zh: 國家棄憲如孤皺腐鼠耳。

translations.literal: The state discarding Xian is like discarding lone-chick rotten rat ear.
translations.idiomatic: The court could cast you off as easily as a sick fledgling or a rotting rat.'

sentences.zh: 』憲大震懼，皇后為毀服深謝，良久乃得解，使以田還主。

translations.literal: ' Xian greatly trembled in fear; the empress for him destroyed garments deep apologizing—long time then obtained resolution—ordered return fields to princess.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian was terrified. The empress shed her formal robes and interceded at length until the emperor relented and ordered the land returned to the princess.

sentences.zh: 雖不繩其罪，然亦不授以重任。

translations.literal: Although did not bind his crime, yet also did not confer heavy responsibility.
translations.idiomatic: He was not punished by law, yet neither was he given serious office.

sentences.zh: 和帝即位，太后臨朝，憲以侍中，內幹機密，出宣誥命。

translations.literal: When Emperor He ascended, the empress dowager faced court; Xian as palace attendant inwardly managed secrets, outward proclaimed edicts.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor He the dowager held court. Dou Xian, as palace attendant, controlled confidential business within and published edicts without.

sentences.zh: 肅宗遺詔以篤為虎賁中郎將，篤弟景、瑰並中常侍，於是兄弟皆在親要之地。

translations.literal: Emperor Zhang's testament edict made Du as Colonel of the Gentlemen of the Household for All Purposes; Du's younger brothers Jing and Gui both were palace attendants—thereupon brothers all were in intimate critical positions.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Zhang's will named Dou Du colonel of the imperial guard; Dou Jing and Dou Gui became eunuch attendants, so every brother held a post close to power.

sentences.zh: 憲以前太尉鄧彪有義讓。

translations.literal: Xian because former Grand Commandant Deng Biao had righteous yielding.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian singled out the former grand commandant Deng Biao for his reputation for modest self-effacement.

sentences.zh: 先帝所敬，而仁厚委隨，故尊崇之，以為太傅，令百官總己以聽。

translations.literal: Honored by former emperor, and benevolent thick yielding easy—therefore honored revered him, making Grand Tutor, ordered hundred officials total self to listen.
translations.idiomatic: A man the late emperor had respected, Deng Biao was pliant and good-natured; Dou Xian exalted him as grand tutor and told the bureaucracy to defer to him in everything.

sentences.zh: 其所施為，輒外令彪奏，內白太后，事無不從。

translations.literal: What he enacted, outwardly ordered Biao memorialize, inwardly reported empress dowager—affairs none not followed.
translations.idiomatic: Whatever Dou Xian wanted he had Deng Biao memorialize abroad while he advised the dowager within—nothing was refused.

sentences.zh: 又屯騎校尉桓郁，累世帝師，而性和退自守，故上書薦之，令授經禁中。

translations.literal: Also Colonel of Garrison Cavalry Huan Yu—accumulated generations imperial teacher—and nature mild retiring self-guarding—therefore submitted memorial recommending him, ordered teach classics in forbidden center.
translations.idiomatic: Huan Yu of the garrison cavalry came from a line of tutors to the throne and was mild and retiring; Dou Xian recommended him to lecture on the classics inside the palace.

sentences.zh: 所以內外協附，莫生疑異。

translations.literal: Therefore inside outside coordinated attached—none bore doubt difference.
translations.idiomatic: Court and camp alike aligned with him and voiced no dissent.

sentences.zh: 憲性果急，睚眥之怨莫不報復。

translations.literal: Xian's nature decisive impatient—all grievance of a sidelong glance did not fail revenge.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian was abrupt and vindictive; he repaid the slightest slight.

sentences.zh: 初，永平時，謁者韓紆嘗考劾父勛獄，憲遂令客斬紆子，以首祭勛冢。

translations.literal: Initially in Yongping times, usher Han Xu once examined impeached father Xun's prison—Xian thereupon ordered retainers behead Xu's son, with head sacrifice Xun's tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Years before, the usher Han Xu had prosecuted Dou Xun; Dou Xian now had a client murder Han Xu's son and offer the head at Dou Xun's grave.

sentences.zh: 齊殤王子都鄉侯暢來吊國憂，暢素行邪僻，與步兵校尉鄧疊親屬數往來京師，因疊母元自通長樂宮，得幸太后，被詔召詣上東門。

translations.literal: Prince of Qi Shang's son Marquis of Duxiang, Chang, came to condole state's mourning; Chang's plain conduct was wicked perverse; with Colonel of Foot Soldiers Deng Die's kin several times went came capital; through Die's mother Yuan self-connected to Changle Palace—obtained favor empress dowager—received edict summon to Upper East Gate.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Chang, marquis of Duxiang and son of the prince of Qi who died young, came to mourn the national loss. He was a dissolute man who kept company with the relatives of Deng Die, colonel of foot soldiers, in the capital. Through Deng Die's mother, Yuan, he won access to Changle Palace, caught the dowager's eye, and received a summons to the Upper East Gate.

sentences.zh: 憲懼見幸，分宮省之權，遣客刺殺暢於屯衛之中，而歸罪於暢弟利侯剛，乃使侍御史與青州刺史雜考剛等。

translations.literal: Xian feared seeing favor—dividing palace province's power—sent retainers assassinate Chang within garrison guards—and blamed Chang's younger brother Marquis of Li Gang—then ordered attendant censor with Qing province governor mixed examine Gang etc.
