translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian feared Liu Chang would win favor and dilute his hold on the inner court. He had Chang murdered amid the guards, pinned the crime on Chang's brother Marquis Gang of Li, and set the attendant censors and the governor of Qing province on Gang's trail.

sentences.zh: 後事發覺，太后怒，閉憲於內宮。

translations.literal: Afterward affair discovered; empress dowager angry—closed Xian in inner palace.
translations.idiomatic: When the plot surfaced the dowager in her rage confined Dou Xian inside the palace.

sentences.zh: 憲懼誅，自求擊匈奴以贖死。

translations.literal: Xian feared execution—self sought strike Xiongnu to redeem death.
translations.idiomatic: Facing execution, Dou Xian volunteered to lead a punitive expedition against the Xiongnu to buy his life.

sentences.zh: 會南單于請兵北伐，乃拜憲車騎將軍，金印紫綬，官屬依司空，以執金吾耿秉為副，發北軍五校、黎陽、雍營、緣邊十二郡騎士，及羌胡兵出塞。

translations.literal: Meeting Southern Shanyu requested troops north campaign—thereupon appointed Xian chariots and cavalry general, gold seal purple cord—officials followed Grand Minister of Works—with Bearer of the Mace Geng Bing as deputy—deployed Northern Army five colonels, Liyang, Yong camp, twelve frontier commanderies' knights, and Qiang Hu troops out passes.
translations.idiomatic: The southern Shanyu asked for a northern campaign at just that moment, so Dou Xian received the chariots-and-cavalry general's gold seal and purple ribbon with a staff matching the Grand Minister of Works. Geng Bing served as deputy. Troops included the five colonels of the northern army, the Liyang and Yong camps, knights from twelve border commanderies, and Qiang and Hu auxiliaries.

sentences.zh: 明年，憲與秉各將四千騎及南匈奴左谷蠡王師子萬騎出朔方雞鹿塞，南單于屯屠河，將萬餘騎出滿夷谷，度遼將軍鄧鴻及緣邊義從羌胡八千騎，與左賢王安國萬騎出稒陽塞，皆會涿邪山。

translations.literal: Next year Xian and Bing each led four thousand riders and Southern Xiongnu Left Guli king Shizi ten thousand riders out Shuofang Jilu Pass; Southern Shanyu stationed on Tutu River led over ten thousand riders out Manyi Valley; General Who Crosses the Liao Deng Hong and frontier volunteer Qiang Hu eight thousand riders with Left Worthy King Anguo ten thousand riders out Guyang Pass—all met at Zhuoye Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: The next year Dou Xian and Geng Bing each took four thousand horsemen and ten thousand riders under the southern Xiongnu Left Guli king Shizi through Jilu Pass at Shuofang. The southern Shanyu camped on the Tutu River and sent more than ten thousand out Manyi Valley. Deng Hong, general who crosses the Liao, brought eight thousand frontier auxiliaries alongside ten thousand under the Left Worthy King Anguo through Guyang Pass. All converged on Mount Zhuoye.

sentences.zh: 憲分遣副校尉閻盤、司馬耿夔、耿譚將左谷蠡王師子、右呼衍王須訾等，精騎萬餘，與北單于戰於稽落山，大破之，虜眾崩潰，單于遁走，追擊諸部，遂臨私渠比鞮海。

translations.literal: Xian separately dispatched Vice Colonel Yan Pan, majors Geng Kui and Geng Tan leading Left Guli king Shizi, Right Huyan king Zuzi etc.—picked riders over ten thousand—with Northern Shanyu battled at Jiluo Mountain, greatly broke them; captive crowds collapsed; Shanyu fled; pursued struck various departments—thereupon reached Sibi Bidi Sea.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian detached Yan Pan, Geng Kui, and Geng Tan with more than ten thousand picked horsemen under Shizi and the Right Huyan king. They smashed the northern Shanyu at Mount Jiluo; his host scattered and he fled. The pursuit drove through tribe after tribe to Lake Sibi Bidi.

sentences.zh: 斬名王以下萬三千級，獲生口馬、牛、羊、橐駝百餘萬頭。

translations.literal: Beheaded named kings and below thirteen thousand grades; obtained captives horses oxen sheep camels over a million head.
translations.idiomatic: They took thirteen thousand heads from named kings downward and seized over a million head of livestock and prisoners.

sentences.zh: 於是溫犢須、曰逐、溫吾、夫渠王柳鞮等八十一部率眾降者，前後二十餘萬人。

translations.literal: Thereupon Wendu Xu, Rizhu, Wenwu, Fuqu king Liudi etc. eighty-one divisions leading crowds surrendering—before after over two hundred thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: Eighty-one tribes led by chiefs such as Wendu Xu, Rizhu, Wenwu, and Fuqu king Liudi submitted in waves totaling more than two hundred thousand people.

sentences.zh: 憲、秉遂登燕然山，去塞三千餘里，刻石勒功，紀漢威德，令班固作銘曰：

translations.literal: Xian, Bing thereupon ascended Yanran Mountain—departed passes over three thousand li—carved stone inscribe merit—record Han might virtue—ordered Ban Gu compose inscription saying:
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian and Geng Bing climbed Yanran Mountain, over three thousand li beyond the frontier, and had their victory carved in stone; Ban Gu was ordered to compose the inscription:

sentences.zh: 鑠王師兮征荒裔，剿凶虐兮截海外，敻其邈兮亙地界，封神丘兮建隆曷，熙帝載兮振萬世。

translations.literal: Bright royal army ah campaigns distant wastes; exterminates fierce cruelty ah cuts off beyond seas; vast its remoteness ah spans earth's bounds; enshrines divine mound ah establishes lofty marker; splendid emperor's reign ah shakes ten thousand generations.
translations.idiomatic: The royal host blazes forth to the ends of the earth; it extirpates cruelty beyond the sea; its reach is vast as the horizon; it marks the sacred mound and raises a towering stone; the emperor's reign resounds through the ages.

sentences.zh: 憲乃班師而還。

translations.literal: Xian thereupon ordered troops withdraw and return.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian then ordered the army home.

sentences.zh: 遣軍司馬吳汜、梁諷，奉金帛遺北單于，宣明國威，而兵隨其後。

translations.literal: Sent army major Wu Si, Liang Feng bearing gold silk as gift to Northern Shanyu—proclaimed manifest state's might—and troops followed behind.
translations.idiomatic: He dispatched the army majors Wu Si and Liang Feng with gold and silk for the northern Shanyu to proclaim Han majesty while troops followed as escort.

sentences.zh: 時虜中乖亂，汜、諷所到，輒招降之，前後萬餘人。

translations.literal: At that time within captives disorder—where Si, Feng arrived—then summoned surrender—before after over ten thousand persons.
translations.idiomatic: With the nomads in confusion, wherever Wu Si and Liang Feng went they gathered surrenders totaling more than ten thousand.

sentences.zh: 遂及單于於西海上，宣國威信，致以詔賜，單于稽首拜受。

translations.literal: Thereupon reached Shanyu on western sea—proclaimed state's trust might—conveyed edict gifts—Shanyu knocked head bowed received.
translations.idiomatic: They overtook the Shanyu on the western sea, proclaimed imperial authority, and presented edicts and gifts; the Shanyu kowtowed and accepted.

sentences.zh: 諷因說宜修呼韓邪故事，保國安人之福。

translations.literal: Feng thereby persuaded ought restore Chanyu's old story—preserve state pacify people's blessing.
translations.idiomatic: Liang Feng urged him to follow the precedent of Huhanye so his people might dwell in peace.

sentences.zh: 單于喜悅，即將其眾與諷俱還，到私渠海，聞漢軍已入塞，乃遣弟右溫禺鞮王奉貢入侍，隨諷詣闕。

translations.literal: Shanyu pleased—immediately led his crowds with Feng together return—reached Sibi Sea—heard Han army already entered passes—thereupon sent younger brother Right Wenduti king offer tribute enter attendance—follow Feng to palace gate.
translations.idiomatic: Pleased, the Shanyu marched back with Liang Feng. At Sibi Sea he learned Han troops had re-entered the passes and sent his brother, the Right Wenduti king, to court with tribute. Liang Feng escorted him to the capital.

sentences.zh: 憲以單于不自身到，奏還其侍弟。

translations.literal: Xian because Shanyu not personally arrived—memorialized return his attendant younger brother.
translations.idiomatic: Because the Shanyu himself had not come, Dou Xian memorialized to send the hostage prince home.

sentences.zh: 南單于於漠北遺憲古鼎，容五斗，其傍銘曰『仲山甫鼎，其萬年子子孫孫永保用』，憲乃上之。

translations.literal: Southern Shanyu on desert north presented Xian ancient tripod—held five dou—inscription at side says 'Zhong Shanfu's tripod—may his myriad years sons grandsons forever protect use'—Xian thereupon presented upward.
translations.idiomatic: The southern Shanyu gave Dou Xian an ancient bronze from the northern desert, holding five dou, inscribed 'The tripod of Zhong Shanfu—may his line forever keep and use it.' Dou Xian presented it to the throne.

sentences.zh: 詔使中郎將持節即五原拜憲大將軍，封武陽侯，食邑二萬戶。

translations.literal: Edict ordered General of the Gentlemen of the Household bearing staff immediately at Wuyuan appoint Xian Grand General—enfeoff Wuyang marquis—food fief twenty thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: An edict sent a general of the gentlemen with credentials to Wuyuan to name Dou Xian grand general and marquis of Wuyang with twenty thousand households.

sentences.zh: 憲固辭封，賜策許焉。

translations.literal: Xian firmly declined enfeoffment—bestowed strategy document permitted.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian refused the title; the emperor issued a written assurance accepting his plea.

sentences.zh: 舊大將軍位在三公下，置官屬依太尉。

translations.literal: Formerly grand general rank below Three Excellencies—set officials following Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Traditionally the grand general had stood below the Three Excellencies with a staff patterned on the grand commandant's.

sentences.zh: 憲威權震朝廷，公卿希旨，奏憲位次太傅下，三公上；

translations.literal: Xian's might authority shook court—dukes ministers hoped intent—memorialized Xian rank order below Grand Tutor—above Three Excellencies;
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian's power dominated the court; lords and ministers read his wishes and proposed that he rank below the grand tutor but above the Three Excellencies;

sentences.zh: 長史、司馬秩中二千石，從事中郎二人六百石，自下各有增。

translations.literal: chief clerk and major's salary middle two thousand piculs—two staff advisers six hundred piculs—below each had increases.
translations.idiomatic: Raising the chief clerk and major to two thousand piculs, two staff advisers to six hundred, with corresponding boosts down the line.

sentences.zh: 振旅還京師。

translations.literal: Rousted army returned capital.
translations.idiomatic: He brought the army home in triumph.

sentences.zh: 於是大開倉府，勞賜士吏，其所將諸郡二千石子弟從征者，悉除太子舍人。

translations.literal: Thereupon greatly opened granaries treasuries—rewarded officers clerks—those he led various commanderies two thousand piculs' sons younger who followed campaign—all appointed heir-apparent houseman.
translations.idiomatic: He threw open the storehouses to reward officers and men and made every son of a two-thousand-picul official who had marched with him a gentleman houseman to the heir apparent.

sentences.zh: 是時，篤為衛尉，景、瑰皆侍中、奉車、駙馬都尉，四家競修第宅，窮極工匠。

translations.literal: At this time Du was Commandant of the Guards; Jing and Gui both palace attendants, bearer of the vitara, and commandant of the consorts for horsemen—the four families vied building mansions—exhausted artisans to the limit.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Du was commandant of the guards; Dou Jing and Dou Gui were eunuch attendants, bearers of the vitara, and commandants for imperial sons-in-law. The four households competed in building mansions and strained every craftsman in Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 明年，詔曰：『大將軍憲，前歲出征，克滅北狄，朝加封賞，固讓不受。

translations.literal: Next year edict said: 'Grand General Xian—former year went out campaigned—conquered destroyed northern Di—court added enfeoff rewards—firmly declined not accept.'
translations.idiomatic: The next year an edict read: 'Grand General Dou Xian destroyed the northern barbarians last year yet steadfastly refused additional honors.'

sentences.zh: 舅氏舊典，並蒙爵士。

translations.literal: Maternal uncles' old precedent—all received noble ranks.
translations.idiomatic: By the ancient precedent for the empress's kin he must receive a noble title.'

sentences.zh: 其封憲冠軍侯。

translations.literal: Enfeoff Xian as marquis who vanquishes champions.
translations.idiomatic: Let Dou Xian be enfeoffed as marquis who vanquishes champions.'

sentences.zh: 邑二萬戶；

translations.literal: Fief twenty thousand households;
translations.idiomatic: His income shall be twenty thousand households;

sentences.zh: 篤郾侯，景汝陽侯，瑰夏陽侯，各六千戶。

translations.literal: Du Marquis of Yan; Jing Marquis of Ruyang; Gui Marquis of Xiayang—each six thousand households.'
translations.idiomatic: Dou Du as marquis of Yan, Dou Jing of Ruyang, and Dou Gui of Xiayang—six thousand households each.'

sentences.zh: 』憲獨不受封，遂將兵出鎮涼州，以侍中鄧疊行征西將軍事為副。

translations.literal: ' Xian alone did not accept enfeoffment—thereupon led troops out garrison Liang province—with palace attendant Deng Die acting west campaign affairs as deputy.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian alone refused his fief and took troops to garrison Liang province with the palace attendant Deng Die as deputy for western operations.

sentences.zh: 北單于以漢還侍弟，復遣車諧儲王等款居延塞，欲入朝見，願請大使。

translations.literal: Northern Shanyu because Han returned attendant younger brother—again sent Chexie Chuwang etc. sincerely Juyan frontier pass—wished enter court see—requested great envoy.
translations.idiomatic: When Han returned the hostage prince, the northern Shanyu sent the king of Chexie Chu and others to camp sincerely at Juyan Pass seeking audience and asking for a senior envoy.

sentences.zh: 憲上遣大將軍中護軍班固行中郎將，與司馬梁諷迎之。

translations.literal: Xian memorialized dispatch grand general's chief of household Ban Gu acting general of the gentlemen with major Liang Feng to welcome him.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian requested Ban Gu, chief of his household, act as general of the gentlemen with major Liang Feng to escort the Shanyu in.

sentences.zh: 會北單于為南匈奴所破，被創遁走，固至私渠海而還。

translations.literal: Meeting Northern Shanyu by Southern Xiongnu broken—wounded fled—Gu reached Sibi Sea and returned.
translations.idiomatic: The northern Shanyu was routed by the southern Xiongnu and fled wounded; Ban Gu reached Sibi Sea and turned back.

sentences.zh: 憲以北虜微弱，遂欲滅之。

translations.literal: Xian because northern captives weak slight—thereupon wished destroy them.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian judged the northern tribes weak and meant to finish them.

sentences.zh: 明年，夏遣右校尉耿夔、司馬任尚、趙博等將兵擊北虜於金微山，大破之，克獲甚眾。

translations.literal: Next year summer dispatched Right Colonel Geng Kui, majors Ren Shang, Zhao Bo etc. led troops strike northern captives at Golden Micro Mountain—greatly broke them—captures very many.
translations.idiomatic: The next summer he sent Geng Kui, Ren Shang, and Zhao Bo against them at Mount Golden Micro and won a crushing victory with heavy captures.

sentences.zh: 北單于逃走，不知所在。

translations.literal: Northern Shanyu fled—did not know where located.
translations.idiomatic: The northern Shanyu vanished and was never found.

sentences.zh: 憲既平匈奴，威名大盛，以耿夔、任尚等為爪牙，鄧疊、郭璜為心腹。

translations.literal: Xian already pacified Xiongnu—prestige name greatly flourished—took Geng Kui, Ren Shang etc. as claws fangs—Deng Die, Guo Huang as heart belly.
translations.idiomatic: With the Xiongnu subdued Dou Xian's fame swelled; Geng Kui and Ren Shang became his enforcers, Deng Die and Guo Huang his confidants.

sentences.zh: 班固、傅毅之徒，皆置幕府，以典文章。

translations.literal: Ban Gu, Fu Yi and sorts—all placed headquarters—by which regulate literary compositions.
translations.idiomatic: Ban Gu, Fu Yi, and others sat in his bureau drafting documents.

sentences.zh: 刺史、守令多出其門。

translations.literal: Regional inspectors and governors mostly exited his gate.
translations.idiomatic: Most provincial governors and commandery magistrates owed him their posts.

sentences.zh: 尚書僕射郅壽、樂恢並以忤意，相繼自殺。

translations.literal: Imperial Secretary Vice Director Zhi Shou, Le Hui both because offended intent—mutually followed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial secretary vice directors Zhi Shou and Le Hui both crossed him and killed themselves in succession.

sentences.zh: 由是朝臣震懾，望風承旨。

translations.literal: Thereby court ministers trembled—gazed wind received intent.
translations.idiomatic: Court officials trembled and rushed to do his bidding.

sentences.zh: 而篤進位特進，得舉吏，見禮依三公。

translations.literal: And Du advanced rank Specially Advanced—obtained recommend clerks—received ritual following Three Excellencies.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Du rose to Specially Advanced, gained the right to recommend officials, and received honors matching the Three Excellencies.

sentences.zh: 景為執金吾，瑰光祿勛，權貴顯赫，傾動京都。

translations.literal: Jing as Bearer of the Mace—Gui Superintendent of the Imperial Household—power noble blazing—shook moving capital.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Jing became bearer of the mace and Dou Gui superintendent of the imperial household; their swagger rocked the capital.

sentences.zh: 雖俱驕縱，而景為尤甚，奴客緹騎依倚形勢，侵陵小人，強奪財貨，篡取罪人，妻略婦女。

translations.literal: Although all arrogant unrestrained—yet Jing was especially severe—slave retainers cavalry escorts relied leaning situation—invaded humiliate petty men—forcibly seized wealth goods—usurp took criminals—seized women wives.
translations.idiomatic: All were insolent, but Dou Jing worst: retainers and mounted guards abused their master's power to bully commoners, seize property, free condemned men, and abduct women.

sentences.zh: 商賈閉塞，如避寇仇。

translations.literal: Merchants shut blocked—as avoiding bandit foes.
translations.idiomatic: Merchants barred their shops as if fleeing bandits.

sentences.zh: 有司畏懦，莫敢舉奏。

translations.literal: Responsible offices fearful cowardly—none dared memorialize impeach.
translations.idiomatic: Magistrates were too frightened to present charges.

sentences.zh: 太后聞之，使謁者策免景官，以特進就朝位。

translations.literal: Empress dowager heard it—sent usher document dismiss Jing office—with Specially Advanced attend court seat.
translations.idiomatic: The dowager learned of it and stripped Dou Jing of office by edict while letting him keep Specially Advanced rank at court.

sentences.zh: 瑰少好經書，節約自修，出為魏郡，遷潁川太守。

translations.literal: Gui young loved classics texts—frugal self-cultivate—went out as Wei commandery—promoted Yingchuan governor.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gui had loved the classics in youth and lived modestly; he served as governor of Wei commandery and then Yingchuan.

sentences.zh: 竇氏父子兄弟並居列位，充滿朝廷。

translations.literal: Dou clan fathers sons brothers together occupied arrayed positions—filled court.
translations.idiomatic: Fathers, sons, and brothers of the Dou house packed the bureaucracy.

sentences.zh: 叔父霸為城門校尉，霸弟褒將作大匠，褒弟嘉少府，其為侍中、將、大夫、郎吏十餘人。

translations.literal: Uncle Ba as Colonel of the City Gates—Ba's younger brother Bao as Grand Adjunct of Works—Bao's younger brother Jia as Privy Treasurer—those who were palace attendants, generals, grandees, gentlemen over ten persons.
translations.idiomatic: Their uncle Dou Ba was colonel of the gates; Dou Bao, grand adjunct of works; Dou Jia, privy treasurer—more than ten further Dou kinsmen served as attendants, generals, and gentlemen.

sentences.zh: 憲既負重勞，陵肆滋甚。

translations.literal: Xian already bore heavy toil—insolent unrestrained daily increased.
translations.idiomatic: Flush with victory, Dou Xian grew ever more overbearing.

sentences.zh: 四年，封鄧疊為穰侯。

translations.literal: Fourth year, enfeoffed Deng Die as Marquis of Rang.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year Deng Die was enfeoffed as marquis of Rang.

sentences.zh: 疊與其弟步兵校尉磊及母元，又憲女婿射聲校尉郭舉，舉父長樂少府璜，皆相交結。

translations.literal: Die with his younger brother Colonel of Foot Soldiers Lei and mother Yuan—also Xian's son-in-law Colonel of the Shooters Gu Ju—Ju's father Privy Treasurer of Changle Gu Huang—all mutually intersected linked.
translations.idiomatic: Deng Die, his brother Lei who was colonel of foot soldiers, their mother Yuan, Dou Xian's son-in-law Gu Ju who commanded the shooters at the ready, and Gu Ju's father Gu Huang, privy treasurer of Changle, formed a tight clique.

sentences.zh: 元、舉並出入禁中，舉得幸太后，遂共圖為殺害。

translations.literal: Yuan, Ju both went came forbidden center—Ju obtained favor empress dowager—thereupon together plotted to murder.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan and Gu Ju moved freely in the inner palace; Gu Ju won the dowager's favor, and the cabal plotted assassination.

sentences.zh: 帝陰知其謀，乃與近幸中常侍鄭眾定議誅之。

translations.literal: Emperor secretly knew their plot—then with intimate favored palace attendant Zheng Zhong fixed plan execute them.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor He learned of the plot and, with his trusted eunuch Zheng Zhong, laid plans to destroy them.

sentences.zh: 以憲在外，慮其懼禍為亂，忍而未發。

translations.literal: Because Xian outside—worried his fear disaster become chaos—endured yet not issued.
translations.idiomatic: Because Dou Xian was still in the field the emperor feared a revolt and waited.

sentences.zh: 會憲及鄧疊班師還京師，詔使大鴻臚持節郊迎，賜軍吏各有差。

translations.literal: Meeting Xian and Deng Die ordered troops return capital—edict ordered Grand Herald Bearer of the Mace suburban welcome—bestowed army clerks each had gradation.
translations.idiomatic: When Dou Xian and Deng Die marched back the emperor sent the grand herald with credentials to greet them outside the city and rewarded the officers by rank.

sentences.zh: 憲等既至，帝乃幸北宮，詔執金吾，五校尉勒兵屯衛南、北宮、閉城門，收捕疊、磊、璜、舉，皆下獄誅，家屬自徙合浦。

translations.literal: Xian etc. already arrived—emperor then visited North Palace—ordered Bearer of the Mace—five colonels bind troops garrison guard south north palaces—closed city gates—arrested Die, Lei, Huang, Ju—all down prison executed—families self moved Hepu.
translations.idiomatic: Once they arrived the emperor went to the North Palace, ordered the bearer of the mace and five colonels to seal the gates and hold both palaces. Deng Die, Lei, Gu Huang, and Gu Ju were seized, executed, and their kin banished to Hepu.

sentences.zh: 遣謁者僕射收憲大將軍印綬，更封為冠軍侯。

translations.literal: Sent Usher Director retrieve Xian grand general seals cords—again enfeoff as marquis who vanquishes champions.
translations.idiomatic: An usher director took Dou Xian's grand general's seals and re-enfeoffed him nominally as marquis who vanquishes champions.

sentences.zh: 憲及篤、景、瑰皆遣就國。

translations.literal: Xian and Du, Jing, Gui all dispatched go to kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian, Dou Du, Dou Jing, and Dou Gui were sent to their fiefs.

sentences.zh: 帝以太后故，不欲名誅憲，為選嚴能相督察之。

translations.literal: Emperor because empress dowager reason—did not wish openly execute Xian—for selected stern capable chancellor supervise inspect them.
translations.idiomatic: Out of respect for the dowager the emperor avoided an open execution of Dou Xian and instead chose stern administrators to watch him.

sentences.zh: 憲、篤、景到國，皆迫令自殺，宗族，賓客以憲為官者皆免歸本郡。

translations.literal: Xian, Du, Jing reached kingdoms—all forced ordered suicide—clan kin, retainers who because Xian held office all dismissed return native commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: At their fiefs Dou Xian, Dou Du, and Dou Jing were forced to kill themselves; kin and clients who had held office through Dou Xian were stripped and sent home.

sentences.zh: 瑰以素自修，不被逼迫，明年坐稟假貧人，徙封羅侯，不得臣吏人。

translations.literal: Gui because plain self-cultivated—not pressed compelled—next year convicted for grain-relief fraud harming poor people—removed enfeoff Marquis of Luo—not obtain minister clerks people.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gui, known for rectitude, was not driven to suicide at first; the next year he lost his fief for corrupt grain relief to the poor and was demoted to marquis of Luo without authority over officials or commoners.

sentences.zh: 初，竇後之譖梁氏，憲等豫有謀焉，永元十年，梁棠兄弟徙九真還，路由長沙，逼瑰令自殺。

translations.literal: Initially when Empress Dou slandered Liang clan—Xian etc. beforehand had plotted—tenth year Yongyuan—Liang Tang brothers moved Jiuzhen return—road passed Changsha—forced Gui order suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Long before, when Empress Dou had framed the Liang clan, Dou Xian had been party to it. In Yongyuan 10 the Liang brothers returning from exile in Jiuzhen passed through Changsha and forced Dou Gui to take his own life.

sentences.zh: 後和熹鄧後臨朝，永初三年，詔諸竇前歸本郡者與安豐侯萬全俱還京師。

translations.literal: Afterward Empress Dowager Hexi Deng faced court—third year Yongchu—edict ordered various Dou previously returned native commanderies together with Marquis of Anfeng Wanquan all return capital.
translations.idiomatic: Later, under Empress Dowager Deng of Hexi, Yongchu 3 saw an edict summoning Dou kin banished to their commanderies back to the capital together with Dou Wanquan, marquis of Anfeng.

sentences.zh: 萬全少子章。

translations.literal: Wanquan's youngest son Zhang.
translations.idiomatic: Among them was Zhang, youngest son of Dou Wanquan.

sentences.zh: 論曰：衛青、霍去病資強漢之眾，連年以事匈奴，國耗太半矣，而猾虜未之勝，所世猶傳其良將，豈非以身名自終邪！

translations.literal: Discourse says: Wei Qing and Huo Qubing deployed mighty Han's masses year after year against the Xiongnu until the treasury lost more than half—yet those wily nomads were never fully subdued—and posterity still calls them fine generals. Is that not because they kept their lives and good names to the end?
translations.idiomatic: The historian comments: Wei Qing and Huo Qubing spent half the empire's wealth on yearly campaigns against the Xiongnu without final victory, yet history still remembers them as great commanders—perhaps because they died with honor intact.

sentences.zh: 竇憲率羌胡邊雜之師，一舉而空朔庭，至乃追奔稽落之表，飲馬比鞮之曲，銘石負鼎，薦告清廟。

translations.literal: Dou Xian led a motley army of Qiang, Hu, and frontier auxiliaries and in one blow emptied the northern court; he pursued the rout beyond Mount Jiluo, watered horses in the bend of the Bidi, carved his victory in stone, carried the sacred cauldron, and reported to the Clear Temple.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian took a mixed host of Qiang, Hu, and border troops, swept the northern court in a single stroke, chased the enemy past Jiluo, watered his horses at Bidi, cut inscriptions in stone, and offered the victory at the ancestral shrine.

sentences.zh: 列其功庸，兼茂於前多矣，而後世莫稱者，章末釁以降其實也。

translations.literal: Set forth his merit and service—it was richer than the forebears'—yet later ages do not praise him, for the final stain cut his achievement down to size.
translations.idiomatic: His record of service outshone those who came before, but later ages pass him in silence because the guilt that closed his life drags the whole story down.

sentences.zh: 是以下流，君子所甚惡焉。

translations.literal: Therefore the lower course is what the gentleman most detests.
translations.idiomatic: That is why a base end is what every gentleman loathes.

sentences.zh: 夫二三子是之不過房幄之間，非復搜揚仄陋，選舉而登也。

translations.literal: As for these few, their rise did not pass beyond the bedchamber curtains; it was no longer a search in humble corners and selection by merit that raised them.
translations.idiomatic: These men rose through inner-palace favor, not by talent spotted in some forgotten corner of the realm.

sentences.zh: 當青病奴仆之時，竇將軍念咎之日，乃庸力之不暇，思鳴之無晨，何意裂膏腴，享崇號乎？

translations.literal: When Qing was a frail houseborn slave and the day the General Dou faced his reckoning, they had no spare strength for menial toil, no rooster to crow the dawn—how should they have dreamed of carving out rich fiefs and bearing exalted titles?
translations.idiomatic: When Wei Qing was a household slave and Dou Xian faced ruin, they could barely keep body and soul together, let alone picture themselves lords of fat manors and noble names.

sentences.zh: 東方朔稱『用之則為虎，不用則為鼠』，信矣。

translations.literal: Dongfang Shuo said 《Employ him and he is a tiger; set him aside and he is a rat》—truly so.
translations.idiomatic: Dongfang Shuo observed 《Employ him and he is a tiger; set him aside and he is a rat》—words that ring true.

sentences.zh: 以此言之，士有懷琬琰以就煨塵者，亦何可支哉！

translations.literal: By this token, when a man who holds fine jade in his heart ends in embers and dust, what can hold him up!
translations.idiomatic: From this, when a man of jade integrity is ground into the ash, who can bear to look?

sentences.zh: 章字伯向。

translations.literal: Zhang, courtesy name Baixiang.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Zhang, whose courtesy name was Baixiang.

sentences.zh: 少好學，有文章，與馬融、崔瑗同好，更相推薦。

translations.literal: In youth he loved study and had literary talent; with Ma Rong and Cui Yuan he shared the same interests and they recommended one another.
translations.idiomatic: In his youth he was a eager student and a fine writer; he and Ma Rong and Cui Yuan, who shared his tastes, traded recommendations.

sentences.zh: 永初中，三輔遭羌寇，章避難東國，家於外黃。

translations.literal: In the midst of the Yongchu era the Three Adjuncts suffered Qiang raids; Zhang fled east and settled his household at Waihuang.
translations.idiomatic: Midway through Yongchu the capital region was raided by Qiang; Dou Zhang took refuge in the eastern provinces and made his home at Waihuang.

sentences.zh: 居貧，蓬戶蔬食，躬勒孝養，然講然不輟。

translations.literal: He lived in poverty—rush door, coarse food—yet personally saw to filial care for his parents; still his lecturing never ceased.
translations.idiomatic: Though he dwelt in a hovel on coarse fare and tended his parents himself, he never stopped teaching.

sentences.zh: 太僕鄧康聞其名，請欲與交，章不肯往，康以此益重焉。

translations.literal: Grand Coachman Deng Kang heard his name and asked to befriend him; Zhang would not go to him, and Kang for this esteemed him all the more.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Coachman Deng Kang sought his friendship; Dou Zhang declined to call on him, and Deng Kang respected him the more for it.

sentences.zh: 是時學者稱東觀為老氏臧室，道家蓬萊山，康遂薦章人東觀為校書郎。

translations.literal: At that time scholars called the Eastern Library Laozi's treasury and the Daoists' Mount Penglai; Kang therefore recommended Zhang to enter the Eastern Library as gentleman collator of texts.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars likened the Eastern Library to Laozi's treasury and Mount Penglai; Deng Kang recommended Dou Zhang there as a collator of the classics.

sentences.zh: 順帝初，章女年十二，能屬文，以才貌選入掖庭，有寵，與梁皇后並為貴人。

translations.literal: At the beginning of Shundi's reign Zhang's daughter, twelve years old, could compose essays; for talent and beauty she was chosen for the Yeting and won favor, becoming an honored lady alongside Empress Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Emperor Shun's reign Dou Zhang's twelve-year-old daughter could already write; her gifts and looks won her a place in the harem as an honored lady beside Empress Liang.

sentences.zh: 擢章為羽林郎將，遷屯騎校尉。

translations.literal: Zhang was promoted to colonel of the Forest of Plumes and then to colonel of garrison cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Zhang rose to colonel of the Forest of Plumes and then to colonel of garrison cavalry.

sentences.zh: 章謙虛下士。

translations.literal: Zhang was modest and humble toward scholars.
translations.idiomatic: He treated scholars with modest deference.

sentences.zh: 收進時輩，甚得名譽。

translations.literal: He recruited men of his generation and won great reputation.
translations.idiomatic: He drew in his contemporaries and earned wide esteem.

sentences.zh: 是時，梁、竇並貴，各有賓客，多交構其間，章推心待之，故得免於患。

translations.literal: At this time the Liang and Dou houses both were powerful; each had retainers who often sowed discord between them; Zhang treated all with open heart and thus escaped harm.
translations.idiomatic: While the Liang and Dou factions each employed clients who traded barbs across the court, Dou Zhang dealt openly with both sides and so avoided disaster.

sentences.zh: 貴人早卒，帝追思之無已，詔史官樹碑頌德，章自為之辭。

translations.literal: The honored lady died young; the emperor could not cease mourning her and ordered the historians to erect a stele praising her virtue; Zhang himself composed the text.
translations.idiomatic: When the honored lady died young the emperor mourned her endlessly. He had historians raise a eulogy stele, and Dou Zhang wrote the inscription himself.

sentences.zh: 貴人歿後，帝禮待之無衰。

translations.literal: After the honored lady died the emperor's courteous treatment of him did not wane.
translations.idiomatic: After her death the emperor's courtesy toward Dou Zhang never slackened.

sentences.zh: 永和五年，遷少府。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Yonghe he was promoted to privy treasurer.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth Yonghe year he became privy treasurer.

sentences.zh: 漢安二年，轉大鴻臚。

translations.literal: In the second year of Han'an he was transferred to grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: In the second Han'an year he moved to grand herald.

sentences.zh: 建康元年，梁后稱制，章自免，卒於家。

translations.literal: In the first year of Jiankang Empress Liang held the regency; Zhang resigned office of his own accord and died at home.
translations.idiomatic: In the first Jiankang year, under Empress Liang's regency, Dou Zhang retired of his own accord and died at home.

sentences.zh: 中子唐，有俊才，官至虎賁中郎將。

translations.literal: His middle son Tang had outstanding talent and rose to colonel of the Gentlemen of the Household for All Purposes.
translations.idiomatic: His second son, Dou Tang, was brilliantly gifted and reached the colonelcy of the imperial guard.

translations.literal: [Encomium]
translations.idiomatic: Heading marking the laudatory verses.

sentences.zh: 贊曰：悃悃安豐，亦稱才雄。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Plain-hearted lord of Anfeng was likewise hailed a hero of talent.
translations.idiomatic: The encomium reads: Dou Rong of Anfeng, plain and sincere, was yet counted a champion.

sentences.zh: 提契河右，奉圖歸忠。

translations.literal: He grasped the covenant west of the river and offered his maps in loyalty.
translations.idiomatic: He held the west of the river and brought his maps home in fealty.

sentences.zh: 孟孫明邊，伐北開西。

translations.literal: Mengsun clarified the frontier—striking north, opening the west.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Gu, styled Mengsun, secured the borders—north subdued, west opened.

sentences.zh: 憲實空漠，遠兵金山。

translations.literal: Xian truly emptied the desert; distant armies reached Golden Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Dou Xian swept the desert bare and drove the army to Golden Mountain.

sentences.zh: 聽笳龍庭，鏤石燕然。

translations.literal: He listened to reed pipes at the Dragon Court and carved stone on Yanran.
translations.idiomatic: Reeds sounded at the nomad court; his stone stood on Yanran.

sentences.zh: 雖則折鼎，王靈以宣。

translations.literal: Though in the end the sacred tripod shattered, the kingly majesty was thereby proclaimed.
translations.idiomatic: Though the house later broke like a cauldron, royal awe had been made known.
